description: As an egg, this proto-drake was struck by a freak bolt of lightning. Years later, the lightning still lives inside.

name: Storm-Touched Bluefeather
description: Peck. Peck. Zap!

name: Storm-Touched Stomper
description: This what your mother always warned you would happen if you ate grass that was struck by lightning.

name: Water Snake
description: Brought to Orgrimmar aboard merchant ships, these short-lived reptiles are considered pests by the city's residents.

name: Darkmoon Hatchling
description: Most of Violet's numerous forest strider hatchlings are turned into Faire fare by Stamp Thunderhorn, though a few are raised as mounts or purchased by adventurers looking for a petite purple pet.

name: Twilight Spider
description: Twisted by the Twilight Hammer's dark magic, these sinister critters are known to feed on their own young.

name: Twilight Iguana
description: Although most reptiles enjoy basking in sunlight, these scaly beasts love bathing in the glow of Azeroth's twin moons.

name: Fawn
description: Pay attention to deer crossings. Even a fawn will total a goblin trike.

name: Cinder Kitten
description: A playful, purring pyromaniac. Don't pet these kittens without fireproof mittens!

name: Deviate Hatchling
description: The demand for this purple-skinned breed has drastically reduced the raptor population near the Wailing Caverns.

name: Mr. Grubbs
description: The grubs of the Plaguelands are a side effect of necromantic corruption and the massive number of corpses there. Mr. Grubbs is from a particularly acrobatic breed.

name: Venomspitter Hatchling
description: These eight-legged critters have lurked in the shadowy cliffs of the Stonetalon Mountains for millennia.

name: Crow
description: While smaller than the infamous ravens of Gilneas, crows are no less clever or cunning.

name: Parrot
description: Parrots can mimic speech, but have difficulty with meaningful conversation.

name: Crested Owl
description: Many owls nest high in the stone structures of Darnassus, hunting any pests that enter the night elves' city.

name: Shimmering Wyrmling
description: This extremely rare species of mana wyrm is prized for its unique coloration and even temperament.

name: Darkmoon Cub
description: This snuggly kitten loves to frolic, but sometimes it seems to be chasing things that aren't really there.

name: Sunreaver Micro-Sentry
description: Loyal to the Sunreavers from creation, these pint-sized constructs can still pack a punch.

name: Electrified Razortooth
description: Lightning can be seen flowing across its body, teeth to tail.

name: Zandalari Kneebiter
description: This unique raptor was born on the island of Zandalar and transported here along with the rest of the Zandalari army.

name: Rocket Chicken
description: When the Burning Legion invaded Outland, Landro Longshot contributed this design to the war effort. Both the Alliance and Horde passed on production.

name: Scarab Hatchling
description: Spawn of the nefarious Shrieker Scarabs of Ahn'Qiraj, the hatchlings are much more docile (and quiet) than their parents.

name: Stunted Shardhorn
description: The fierce wolvar hunt these ill-tempered brutes for their horns, which are then carved into talismans and other trinkets.

name: Devouring Maggot
description: Howling Fjord teems with the ever shifting cycle of life. When a creature dies, its body is quickly consumed by maggots, which in turn pass those nutrients on to a number of other creatures.

name: Oily Slimeling
description: These living blobs of ooze lack even the most rudimentary form of intelligence, but that doesn't mean they can't be your friend.

name: Ashleaf Spriteling
description: The Ashleaf Sprites of the Timeless Isle tirelessly protect the vegetation of the island from the burning fury of Ordos and his minions.

name: Dandelion Frolicker
description: While fascinated by flowers, the frolicker has also been known to dance with blades of grass and the occasional weed.

name: Alterac Brew-Pup
description: A mastiff bred for rescue, the Alterac brewhound's bloodline is famous for having found and resuscitated many mountain travelers trapped beneath the snow... and then slobbering all over them.

name: Death Adder Hatchling
description: Late in life, if the environmental conditions are correct, a Death Adder may shed its skin and undergo a transformation into an Imperial Python.

name: Murkalot
description: We're Crusaders from a fable. We dance whene'er we're able. We do routines and chorus scenes, with footwork impeccable!

name: Droplet of Y'Shaarj
description: A small remnant of the power of Y'shaarj that spawned the Sha, a single Drop of Y'shaarj can magnify emotional states for thousands of yards, causing entire towns to shut down in fear or doubt.

name: Gooey Sha-ling
description: Sha-lings reflect the emotional state of their handler, becoming agitated, depressed, fearful, or doubtful depending on the magnitude of negative thoughts around them.

name: Kovok
description: The tiny terror of the Dread Wastes, Kovok prefers a steady diet of "brain food:" mushan brains, human brains, any brains really. This strange nutritional requirement may be tangentally related to his enormous head.

name: Spineclaw Crab
description: Spineclaw crabs of Pandaria have existed for thousands of years and remain one of the continent's oldest creatures.

name: Rotten Little Helper
description: Fed up with long hours, low pay, and entitled adventurers whining about sub-standard Winter Veil gifts, these nasty little creatures fled their magical workshops to join up with the Abominable Greench.

name: Bonkers
description: Some young and rambunctious hozen are trained from an early age to use boxing gloves so they don't accidentally kill their tribe members, and more importantly, to keep them from picking their noses.

name: Macabre Marionette
description: These possessed puppets are given a semblance of life by whimsical spirits of the departed.

name: Treasure Goblin
description: Watch your coin purse.

name: Gu'chi Swarmling
description: Swarmlings of Gu'chi spin an ancient, and very powerful, form of silk that has not been seen in Pandaria in ages.

name: Harmonious Porcupette
description: Prolonged exposure to Shaohao's tranquil mists has calmed these spirits, which act as peaceful companions to the Mistweavers.

name: Jadefire Spirit
description: The Jadefire Spirit is thought to be a living embodiment of Yu'lon's own jade breath.

name: Azure Crane Chick
description: Crane chicks of the Timeless Isle sometimes sing a curious song that calms the nerves and soothes the soul.

name: Blackfuse Bombling
description: The first bombling created in the Underhold, Siegecrafter Blackfuse couldn't bear to see it destroyed, and kept it as a friendly, if explosive, pet.

name: Ashwing Moth
description: Jinyu assassins employ this moth as both a portable decoy and a light source to reveal weak spots in their enemy's armor.

name: Sky Lantern
description: A candle inside of the kite heats the air, causing it to rise into the air without the need for wind.

name: Flamering Moth
description: Cats and other curious critters often have short-lived play sessions with this moth that end with a face full of singed hair.

name: Skywisp Moth
description: This curious moth is sometimes found interrupting the spells of a wandering mage or warlock.

name: Ruby Droplet
description: The waters of Ruby Lake are colored a strange reddish hue, possibly due to the ruby crystals found deep within the mountains of the Timeless Isle.

name: Vengeful Porcupette
description: One of the beasts left on the Timeless Isle when it shifted through time, most likely sacrificed by the Ordon to their demigod, Ordos.

name: Jademist Dancer
description: The Jademist Dancers of the Timeless Isle have a curious attraction to the steam vents of the northwestern shore.

name: Ominous Flame
description: The odd blue flames found within the Cavern of Lost Spirits have led many unwary adventurers to their doom.

name: Skunky Alemental
description: When the village of Pi'jiu was attacked by the Ordon Yaungol generations ago, the villagers bravely erected a keg in the center of the village. The following morning, after the celebration had died down, the still-drunken Yaungol blearily agreed to a peace treaty.

name: Gulp Froglet
description: Gulp frogs of the Timeless Isle can feast upon prey several times larger than themselves by slowly digesting the victim externally with a toxic slime.

name: Hogs
description: Tenacious and terrible, the mighty Hogs slaughters weaker enemies without hesitation.

name: Albino Chimaeraling
description: Both heads are equally fearsome on the terrifying chimaera, each capable of its own unique breath attack.

name: Iron Starlette
description: This massive weapon of destruction has been miniaturized for use in the garden.  Moles beware!

name: Dread Hatchling
description: The dark wings will cloud and consume the minds of the weak, because they are just so adorable!

name: Mystical Spring Bouquet
description: Animated by magical means, and chlorophyll.

name: Frostwolf Pup
description: These pups are bundles of fur and teeth until they grow up, at which point they are really just teeth.

name: Bush Chicken
description: Following their introduction to the mainland by the goblins, Bush Chickens have become the entre du jour for Pilgrim's Bounty due to their high deliciousness and low intelligence.

name: Fruit Hunter
description: This anxious avian has a voracious appetite for fruit.

name: Bronze Whelpling
description: The Bronze Whelpling was lost in time until an unusual event allowed her to escape.

name: Ghastly Kid
description: In the ultimate demonstration of stubborness, this once-living nannie simply refused to die.

name: Frostwolf Ghostpup
description:
The little wolf told to stay.
Watching, waiting, for vacant prey.
Too proud to leave, she slipped away.
Unburdened now, forever free to play.

name: Ikky
description: Kaliri hatchlings feast on grubs when they're young, and eventually graduate to anything smaller than themselves.

name: Everbloom Peachick
description: These tiny creatures seem to favor big, fluffy nests, preferably atop an animal-loving dryad.

name: Doom Bloom
description: A clear indication why goblin fertilizers should be used sparingly.

name: Brilliant Bloodfeather
description: The bloodfeather is completely fearless, fanning out its feathers and charging any threat.

name: Golden Dawnfeather
description: It's said the feathers glitter like gold when the rising sun shines upon them.

name: Son of Sethe
description: Godlike being now in travel size.

name: Weebomination
description: Made from bits and pieces of vanquished battle pets, dark magic, and just a pinch of love.

name: Sentinel's Companion
description: The companion of a night elf sentinel, rarely given out as a gift.

name: Puddle Terror
description: Living so closely to the water's surface, the puddle terror is not only friend to all aquatic creatures, it is one.

name: Soul of the Forge
description: When forging enchanted steel, bits of magic seep into the coals.  Over a period of years, decades even, the unbound energy slowly takes form, creating a tiny elemental being of immense fortitude.

name: Sky Fry
description: Playing fetch with a ray is always fun.  You don't even have to aim.

name: Eye of Observation
description: You'll always feel like... something is watching you.

name: Bloodthorn Hatchling
description: Ravagers are favored ogre snacks when they are small and less deadly.

name: Firewing
description: Don't line her cage with paper unless you want a fire.

name: Meadowstomper Calf
description: The intelligence and caring nature of elekk calves make them a stalwart lifelong companion.

name: Moonshell Crab
description: The moonshell crab reflects light so well that they can be picked up and used as a mirror.  Just keep your face away from the claws!

name: Sunfire Kaliri
description: Legend says the Sunfire Kaliri flies into the sun itself when it is ready to die.

name: Autumnal Sproutling
description: From seed to flower, a tiny terror is grown.

name: Forest Sproutling
description: From seed to flower, a tiny terror is grown.

name: Nightshade Sproutling
description: From seed to flower, a tiny terror is grown.

name: Zangar Spore
description: In Draenor, the Zangar Sea seeks to overtake the land by releasing thousands of these spores and slowly growing inland over time.

name: Crimson Spore
description: A spore plucked from the Crimson Fen, an area of Gorgrond that was swallowed when Draenor was torn apart by nether magics.

name: Umbrafen Spore
description: The deep blue Umbrafen would later become home to a tribe of Broken.

name: Seaborne Spore
description: A spore from Zangarra, a central area of the Zangar encroachment that would later bloom into the Zangarmarsh.

name: Elekk Plushie
description: This adorable little elephant is always there for you.

name: Fragment of Suffering
description: It's only mildly irritated.

name: Sun Sproutling
description: From seed to flower, a tiny terror is grown.

name: Leatherhide Runt
description: Many years ago, an ogre tried to use a leatherhide runt as kickball.  He was forever known as Onefoot afterwards.

name: Mud Jumper
description: Orcish children have a tradition where they sneak up on a mud jumper and whisper a secret they wish to come true.  If the mud jumper doesn't jump, the wish will come true.

name: Syd the Squid
description: This tiny squid seems to enjoy wearing pants, but where in the world would you find some that fit?

name: Brilliant Spore
description: Brilliant spores are very rarely seen, found deep under the waves of Draenor within the darkest corners of the Zangar fungal encroachment.

name: Stormwing
description: Half bird, half elemental, the beautiful Stormwing is truly capable of shock and awe.

name: Cursed Birman
description: One of this cat's nine lives has returned to haunt the living by yowling loudly when you're trying to sleep.

name: Teroclaw Hatchling
description: The teroclaw is a potent hunter of the small and furry.

name: Parched Lizard
description: Not only is it quick, it's loud too!

name: Lifelike Mechanical Frostboar
description: After many complaints that taming young boars was a painful experience, engineers came to the rescue with this creepily lifelike model.

name: Widget the Departed
description: Not even death would stop this kitten's insatiable curiosity.

name: Race MiniZep
description: One of the Darkmoon Race's most obnoxious obstacles, now in the palm of your hand!

name: Albino River Calf
description: Their white hide would make them vulnerable to sunburn, if it wasn't as thick as plate armor.

name: Frostfur Rat
description: Annoying and destructive, they can be coaxed with enough cheese to become a tolerable companion.

name: Draenei Micro Defender
description: While these tiny mechanical guardians were created to protect domesticated elekks from pests, they also turned out to be really good at giving back massages.

name: Molten Corgi
description: Loves to herd imps, roll around in lava, and snuggle with elemental lords.

name: River Calf
description: Surprisingly quick when provoked, but easily calmed with a meal of Nagrand arrowbloom.

name: Zomstrok
description: Zomstrok is evil incarnate, the unholy mistake of someone maddened by isolation, a creation that could not be undone.

name: Royal Peachick
description: A single peacock feather is often laid upon a grave to ward off evil spirits.

name: Netherspawn, Spawn of Netherspawn
description: This slime has a long and confusing lineage.

name: Murkidan
description: After consuming the shin bone of Gul'dan, this fiesty demon hunter was caged for 10000 years by his brother Murkfurion.

name: Mossbite Skitterer
description: Camouflaged like moss, the skitterer will hide in trees and leap on their victims from above.

name: Thicket Skitterer
description: Their deceptively cute looks will lure in their prey, and then greet them with a flurry of devastating swipes. Hug with caution.

name: Frostshell Pincher
description: The frostshell pinchers build up layers of ice to give them the extra protection from larger predators.

name: Lovebird Hatchling
description: Of course there are hatchlings, they're called Lovebirds aren't they?

name: Pygmy Cow
description: Originally bred to make milk more palatable for gnomes.

name: Deathwatch Hatchling
description: This hatchling has an unusual sense that allows it to detect and follow along with alpha predators.

name: Icespine Hatchling
description: Equipped with extremely sharp claws, these frigid hatchlings dig through thick layers of ice and rock for shelter.

name: Bone Wasp
description: One ogre ritual involves cupping a bone wasp in their hands and shaking vigorously.  The venom increases strength and invokes vivid imagery.

name: Hatespark the Tiny
description: His hate is not diminished by size.

name: Mechanical Scorpid
description: Iron, iron, steel and fire.  Bellows, gears, plates, and wire.

name: Fen Crab
description: These crafty crustaceans burrow under the mud to avoid being scooped up by larger predators.

name: Crazy Carrot
description: Left to their own devices, carrots will simply lose their minds.

name: Blazing Cindercrawler
description: Tiny, incinerated corpses of various insects line its cage.

name: Amberbarb Wasp
description: Amberbarbs often try to provoke larger creatures to attack it. Once their victim strikes them, they will retaliate with the entire hive.

name: Experiment 13
description: The name implies that there were 12 experiments done before this one that were less effective than sticking some guns onto a mechanical tiger. What could those have possibly been?

name: Bloodsting Wasp
description: Contrary to popular belief, bloodsting wasps do not feed on blood, but love.

name: Wood Wasp
description: Wood wasps make nests in dead or hollowed out trees, and are quick to attack anything that threatens their home.

name: Zangar Crawler
description: They feed off of the nutrient-rich fungal spores which gives these crabs the district coloring of their shells.

name: Grotesque
description: A smaller gargoyle that is used to divert the flow of rainwater on scourge buildings to prevent mausoleum flooding.

name: Fragment of Desire
description: It's only somewhat interested.

name: Sister of Temptation
description: She's the devil on your shoulder, whispering in your ear.

name: Mechanical Axebeak
description: Squeeeeeeeawk!

name: Sea Calf
description: The sea calf has a natural affinity for balancing things.

name: Twilight Wasp
description: In the moonlight their wings create a glittering aura, giving the illusion of twinkling starlight.

name: Brightpaw
description: This magical kitty has so much energy that she will sometimes zip around for no reason whatsoever.

name: Ironclaw Scuttler
description: With claws that can crush a platemail gauntlet, it's best to handle with care.

name: Leviathan Hatchling
description: The dark green water in the Black Temple's sewer system gives these hydras their distinctive color.

name: Ore Eater
description: They eat and eat and eat, until they grow large.  Then they eat some more.

name: Stinkrot
description: What's that smell?

name: Hyjal Wisp
description: Hundreds of these wisps came together to defend Hyjal from Archimonde and save the world.

name: Sky-Bo
description: The Sky-Bo was originally designed by the infamous Millie Watt as a toy for her cat.  The cat just slept in the box next to it.

name: Stonegrinder
description: The master-crafted gear assembly and precision joints in this robot are clearly made with love.

name: Abyssius
description: A fragment of the great abyssal Supremus, Abyssius contains enough fiery essence to burn a hole right through your pocket.

name: Fragment of Anger
description: It's just a little mad.

name: Ancient Nest Guardian
description: The ancient nest guardian was designed with the unthinkable in mind: to hate cats.

name: Lanticore Spawnling
description: No one knows what deep part of Blackwing Descent the Lanticore was kept in, but it probably smelled pretty awful.

name: Lil' Leftovers
description: Cute enough to eat, but not recommended.

name: Stout Alemental
description: The Pandaren Brewmasters accidentally brought a barrel of Uncle Gao's last sentient batch of brew with them to Brewfest.

name: Bloodbeak
description: The ancient tribes of Draenor were known to harvest their sharp beaks for crude tools and weapons.

name: Junglebeak
description: The colorful plumage from these birds are highly sought after by collectors. It is rumored that wearing these feathers will bring you good luck.

name: Axebeak Hatchling
description: The edges of its beak are particularly sharp. Watch your fingers during feeding time!

name: Hydraling
description: The hydraling can be an expensive pet, as it is capable of eating from three food bowls at the same time.

name: Auburn Ringtail
description: Is it three white stripes on a brown tail, or two brown stripes on a white tail?

name: Spring Strider
description: Striders glow brightest during spring, hence the name.

name: Grommloc
description: No matter how much he screams, never give him demon blood.

name: Argi
description: Often caught chewing on things they shouldn't be, goats were initially blamed for the crash of the Exodar.

name: Nethaera's Light
description: The lost magical light of a Dalaran mage, or sentient candle with a positive attitude? You decide.

name: Trunks
description: A magical friend created with equal parts adventure and spirit.

name: Kelp Scuttler
description: They will cover themselves in seaweed and other ocean foliage to get the upper claw on their prey.

name: Cerulean Moth
description: Delicate and dangerous, brilliant and bold, a beauty worth more than gold.

name: Royal Moth
description: Their deep purple wings are highly sought after by ogre magi believing that they can bring prolonged life when added to potions.

name: Dust Bunny
description: This very elusive creature likes to hide in places where many adventurers don't frequently travel.

name: Fetid Waveling
description: After a long night out mages find that their water elementals smell a bit off.

name: Thistleleaf Adventurer
description: Leaving the forest to see the world, which must look so much bigger to such a little guy.

name: Dusty Sporewing
description: Their wings emit a fine powder as a defensive mechanism when it is in danger.

name: Crimsonwing Moth
description: Delicate and dangerous, brilliant and bold, a beauty worth more than its weight in gold.  Too bad they are so light.

name: Swamplighter Firefly
description: Scholars have debated at length about how fireflies glow, but the leading hypothesis is "magic".

name: Violet Firefly
description: These fireflies retain their purple glow by eating only purple flowers and nectar.

name: Sapphire Firefly
description: It has been referred to as "the flying gem" due to its similar luster to the finest of sapphires.

name: Waterfly
description: The watefly uses the surface of the water to reflect the light from their abdomen to attract mates.

name: Fel Pup
description: These pups are bundles of fur and teeth until they grow up, at which point they are really just teeth.

name: Lost Netherpup
description: Rumor has it that some beasts that were touched by the void exist in a space between this world and the next.

name: Cinder Pup
description: Found munching on a blackened bone in the Black Forge of Blackrock Foundry, this pup really seems to warm up to you quickly.

name: Sunblade Micro-Defender
description: Because you don't want to know what happens when a rat wanders into the Sunwell.

name: Chaos Pup
description: The Twisting Nether is an infinite abyss of terrors and one small corner of extreme cuteness.

name: Wretched Servant
description: Magic-addicted elves too small and frail to serve in Kil'Jaeden's army were relegated to servant roles for the higher-class demons.

name: Extinguished Eye
description: What was once a great enemy is now an adorable friend!

name: Hateful Eye
description: What are YOU looking at?!

name: Eye of Inquisition
description: Hello. Is it me you're looking for?

name: Enchanted Pen
description: Strega the Hag, tired of writing thank you notes, used her magic to automate the task.  Unfortunately, the pen had a dark side and all the recipients received strongly worded letters...

name: Emmigosa
description: The great-great-great-great-great granddaughter of Senegos.

name: Stormborne Whelpling
description: Its scales are electrified, making it exceedingly tricky to cuddle.

name: K'ute
description: When this pet is near, calming chimes fill your mind and remind you of a time long passed.

name: Shard of Cyrukh
description: A small amount of Cyrukh burns everlasting within this portion of the Cipher. Best keep it close to you.

name: Tiny Apparition
description: It's more cute than spooky.

name: Risen Saber Kitten
description: Eternally cute.

name: Alarm-o-Bot
description: For Sale: Slightly used Alarm-o-Bot. Rebuilt and refurbished. Still annoying.

name: Dream Whelpling
description: Beautiful dreamer, wake unto me, Starlight and dewdrops are waiting for thee.

name: Nightmare Whelpling
description: What's a little corruption between friends?

name: Burrow Spiderling
description: The front legs of this spider are almost indestructable, allowing it to burrow into almost any surface.

name: Nursery Spider
description: Why is it called a nursery spider? Does it prefer to lurk in nurseries? Why would it do that? What does it do there? Oh, dear! Someone check on the kids!

name: Rose Taipan
description: The last thing you'll see is a streak of red.

name: Savage Cub
description: Pets often look a lot like their owners, which is especially true if the owner was a saberon.

name: Blazing Firehawk
description: Arakkoa sun worship has resulted in the creation of some strange and monstrous minions.

name: Juvenile Scuttleback
description: Juvenile describes this scuttleback's age, not its maturity.

name: Slithershock Elver
description: The rivers of Draenor are filled with moray.  They are vicious predators, attacking anything that enters the water.

name: Young Talbuk
description: Talbuk grow their horns to full size at an early age.

name: Felspider
description: It's just like a regular spider, but fel.

name: Nightmare Bell
description: Once a holy artifact used by draenei priests to calm spirits, it has become animated by anguished souls and drawn to locations of death and corruption.

name: Olivetail Hare
description: Olivetail is a bit of misnomer, as its whole body is just brown...

name: Corrupted Nest Guardian
description: There is very little difference between a corrupted nest guardian and an omelette chef.

name: Long-Eared Owl
description: Its eyesight is better than its hearing.

name: Northern Hawk Owl
description: Its sharp eyes can spot a snowshoe rabbit in a snowstorm.

name: Nightwatch Swooper
description: These creatures can hunt in almost pure darkness.

name: Albatross Chick
description: Many sailors believe that harming an albatross will bring terrible luck upon their voyage.

name: Knockoff Blingtron
description: Don't want to break the bank on that new robot? Have a little helper at half the cost! Come down to Goblin Experimental Engineering Korporation and put in your order! All sales final.

name: Grumpling
description: Grumplings are created when the tears of a kidnapped child fall upon a mound of new snow.

name: Shimmering Aquafly
description: On very rare occasions glowflies synchronize their flashing creating a spectacular sight for any adventurer lucky enough to catch this phenomenon.

name: Terror Larva
description: The terror larva seeks a host to feed on their dreams and terrors. Soon the larva will grow into the host's worst fears.

name: Coralback Fiddler
description: Even in its juvenile stage, this crab's pincers can break bone.

name: Mudshell Conch
description: Guess what its bed is. A piece of bread.

name: Broot
description: I am Broot.

name: Grumpy
description: You saved him, but maybe he saved you.

name: Fledgling Warden Owl
description: A young companion in need of training.

name: Crispin
description: Oddly, he was named Crispin even before the incident.

name: Bleakwater Jelly
description: When a helsquid has devoured most of the food in any area, it can return to its polyp form and begin life anew.

name: Sewer-Pipe Jelly
description: Dalaran's drainage is not as good as you think.

name: Plump Jelly
description: Once its tentacles take hold there is no escape, much like its love for you.

name: Ashmaw Cub
description: This little guy has had a rough start at life, be sure to take good care of him.

name: Ridgeback Piglet
description: He loves to play in the mud! Then he likes to give you a hug!

name: Crusher
description: Found wandering in a small blizzard, this lost goren carries a mining pick to show that he is a jack of all trades.

name: Thaumaturgical Piglet
description: The formula had a bit too much... purple.

name: Energized Manafiend
description: Energized by the ogre-mages of Highmaul, this mana-hungry creature will drain you dry before you know it.

name: Empyreal Manafiend
description: A shimmering creature made of pure arcane energy, it is hard to focus your eyes directly on it.

name: Vicious Broodling
description: You can identify the cruelest and most vicious of the brood by their reddish hue.

name: Slithering Brownscale
description: These snakes are extraordinarily docile creatures.  However, they blend with their environment so well that many adventurers tread too closely to a nest.  Most bites result from totally avoidable incidents.

name: Vale Flitter
description: Vale flitter normally have incredibly long lifespans.  That was until people found use for them in pet battles.

name: Son of Goredome
description: Like father, like son.

name: Left Shark
description: Who would leave such a cute shark behind? The Iron Horde truly has no soul.

name: Legionnaire Murky
description: A proud supporter of the Horde, this tiny murloc left his clutch to seek out blue-clad foes to slay.

name: Spectral Spinner
description: As if a mine full of non-ghost spiders wasn't scary enough.

name: Black-Footed Fox Kit
description: Often it is red-footed after it catches its prey.

name: Mist Fox Kit
description: It disappears as quietly and mysteriously as the mist.

name: Fel Piglet
description: Even piglets are not safe from the Legion's corruption.  Although, this one seems harmless enough.

name: Empowered Manafiend
description: First created in the presence of Highmaul runes, this tiny manafiend really packs a punch!

name: Erudite Manafiend
description: Mages seek these manafiends to absorb their ley energies.

name: Eldritch Manafiend
description: Once a manafiend consumes enough ley energy, it becomes more aggressive and twisted.  Approach with extreme caution.

name: Ghostshell Crab
description: These crabs have developed an advanced form of camouflage that causes their skin to appear translucent.

name: Blorp
description: This fish doesn't seem to respond to your commands at all, or notice that you're there, really.

name: Mudback Calf
description: Surprisingly quick when provoked, but easily calmed with a meal of Nagrand arrowbloom.

name: Flat-Tooth Calf
description: Surprisingly quick when provoked, but easily calmed with a meal of Nagrand arrowbloom.

name: Veilwatcher Hatchling
description: Hunted to near extinction for its feathers, this kaliri has developed keen senses.

name: Echo Batling
description: Most bats are not blind, except this one.  This one is blind.

name: Hog-Nosed Bat
description: Never say "oink" in its presence.

name: Kaliri Hatchling
description: Hunted to near extinction for its feathers, the kaliri has developed keen senses.

name: Glowing Sporebat
description: Wild sporebats are vicious creatures, but if tamed at a young age, they can be loyal companions.

name: Graves
description: In one invasion of the Haunted Mines, a team of heroes only managed to collect a single skull. The golem that resulted, too small to do much damage to the enemy towers, wandered off into the Nexus, eventually finding its way to Azeroth.

name: Ghost Maggot
description: If a live maggot eats the dead, what does an undead maggot eat?

name: Servant of Demidos
description: Demidos created a great many servants when he was hosting parties for other void lords.

name: Shadow Sporebat
description: Wild sporebats are vicious creatures, but if tamed at a young age, they can be loyal companions.

name: Bone Serpent
description: Young bone serpents will try to burrow into a living creature's skull and devour their brain.  So you may want to wear a night cap.

name: Fledgling Kingfeather
description: While other species of birds take turns leading the flock during migration, the kingfeather always remains in the lead.  Whether this is an effort to help the rest of the flock conserve energy or out of dominance remains unknown.

name: Fledgling Oliveback
description: Always in its brother's shadow.

name: Skyhorn Nestling
description: A very rare bird, but this one was intrusted to you.

name: Nibbles
description: This young stalker was easily swayed from Sargeras' grasp by a handful of Magical Mini-Treats.

name: Enchanted Cauldron
description: Strega the Hag enchanted her cauldron to prevent it from tipping over and spilling hot soup.  She never expected it to achieve sentience and attempt to murder her.

name: Enchanted Torch
description: Strega the Hag didn't like carrying her own torches.  One quick enchantment, and the thing would dutifully follow her into the night.

name: Golden Eaglet
description: These birds often prey on turtles by dropping them from great heights to break open their shell.

name: Ghastly Rat
description: By far the best way to spread the plague of undeath.

name: Spiketail Beaver
description: Its traveled all the way from New Beavertown.

name: Crystalline Broodling
description: Its proximity to crystalline mana clusters has hardened this broodlings body to an almost rocklike state.

name: Thornclaw Broodling
description: These creatures have developed a chitin armor around its fangs, legs and abdomen.

name: Leyline Broodling
description: When demons, nightmares, and magic collide!

name: Baby Winston
description: An intelligent ape that claims he is from 'the moon'. Does he mean the White Lady, or the Blue Child?

name: Baby Elderhorn
description: Isn't baby elderhorn an oxymoron?

name: Sunborne Val'kyr
description: Val'kyr are considered "sunborne" when they are honored in the presence of Odyn.

name: Trigger
description: Ever wanted to cuddle with a gun? Introducing Trigger, the Cuter Shooter.

name: Wyrmy Tunkins
description: In his home realm, he commanded an army of ten million demons. Now his power is yours to command. Go make him fight a chicken!

name: Zoom
description: He may not be the fastest snail, but he has the heart of a champion.

name: Celestial Calf
description: How the Mad Merchant got his hands on one of these otherworldly creatures is unknown. What we do know is, he knows it's valuable.

name: Felbat Pup
description: A felbat's fire never burns its master.

name: Corgi Pup
description: His tiny legs make him more aerodynamic.

name: Tinyclaw
description: This bear's necessities are fresh batteries and an occasional oil change.

name: Pygmy Owl
description: No size jokes, please.

name: Sneaky Marmot
description: This marmot has been with you this entire time. He's that good.

name: Orphaned Marsuul
description: It's not really an orphan. The name just makes you feel better about abducting it.

name: Bilescourge
description: The final phase of the bilescourge life cycle.

name: Hungering Claw
description: Thousands of years ago, ten magisters were exiled from Highborne society for seeking power in the corrupting darkness. In the wilds, they found hunger, desperation, and Kosumoth.

name: Munchy
description: A young grovecrawler that has been lovingly cared for by the Hara'ti.

name: Bronze Proto-Whelp
description: The Bronze Proto-Whelps have been discovered only in the most peculiar of timelines.

name: Corrupted Blood
description: Some fragments of Deathwing's blood live on to this day, deep within the lost places of the world, forever seeking their lost host.

name: Unstable Tendril
description: Its friends call it "Slappy."

name: Dreadmaw
description: Deep beneath the Argent Tournament grounds a clutch of eggs belonging to mates Acidmaw and Dreadscale was found.

name: Snobold Runt
description: Really much more tolerable when he's not riding on your head.

name: Crackers
description: They say Fleet Admiral Tethys was never without his trusty parrot by his side - or on his shoulder. Tethys could always call him with a whistle.

name: Paradox Spirit
description: It is a riddle wrapped in a mystery inside an enigma.

name: Rescued Fawn
description: With enough of your love, and tiny milk bottles, she will grow strong enough to rejoin the wild.

name: Coastal Sandpiper
description: It enjoys long walks on the beach.

name: Amalgam of Destruction
description: Infused with the elementium ore used to plate the great dragon Deathwing, and one of the last remnants of the madness that nearly consumed Azeroth.

name: Antoran Bile Larva
description: Proximity to Antorus makes these larva hyper agressive.

name: Nerubian Swarmer
description: Thousands of these skittering nightmares swarm underneath Northrend to this day.

name: Magma Rageling
description: In the eternal balance of frost and fire, the raging flames fuel many aggressive fronts.

name: Ironbound Proto-Whelp
description: The denizens of Ulduar transformed even small whelps into ironclad monstrosities.

name: Runeforged Servitor
description: The runeforged sentries of Ulduar march endlessly without the need for rest or recharging.

name: Son of Skum
description: He's very friendly, but any amount of snuggles is going to result in you getting a nasty shock.

name: Mining Monkey
description: Best keep him away from any cannons.

name: Dig Rat
description: Rogues often kept a few of these in their pockets in times of emergency. Don't ask me why.

name: Mailemental
description: The Postmaster is often described as esoteric, mystical, even inexplicable. For the precious little known about the enigmatic mail-master, perhaps none outside of the wax seal can confirm the existence of the fantastical letter-loving Mailemental.

name: Bile Larva
description: An early stage of the bilescourge life cycle.

name: Sanctum Cub
description: Rub its belly more than twice and you're just asking to be pounced on.

name: Winter Rageling
description: In the eternal balance of frost and fire, the stolid ice forms a defense without measure.

name: Snaplasher
description: Glowing from within, these sprouts have been blessed with a gift from Eonar.

name: G0-R41-0N Ultratonk
description: The next level of scrapbot technology: pushes the scrap into a heap, then destroys it with flame vents and rockets.

name: Dutiful Squire
description: After witnessing your heroism in battle, this squire promised you an oath of fealty.

name: Dutiful Gruntling
description: After witnessing your heroism in battle, this gruntling promised you an oath of fealty.

name: Shadow
description: Lost on Argus and overtaken by darkness, this playful fox can shrug off the void just by being by your side.

name: Blind Rat
description: Do you see how they run?

name: Tinytron
description: During their years-long war with the black dragonflight, the dwarves tried crafting smaller golems that were more difficult to hit.

name: Discarded Experiment
description: Maloriak was nothing if not resourceful, not letting even small scraps of flesh go to waste.

name: Firebat Pup
description: Fire it up!

name: Corgnelius
description: True royalty among Corgis.

name: Creeping Tentacle
description: There is something deeply unsettling about the tentacle, perhaps it is best not to look at it for too long.

name: Boneshard
description: A small fragment of Lord Marrowgar, it will NOT stop assaulting your shins.

name: Blood Boil
description: It really seems to want to touch you, but why?

name: Globe Yeti
description: Who makes them? How do they get there? No one knows, except maybe Greatfather Winter.

name: Docile Skyfin
description: Skyfin can be observed in a wide variety of colors. Biologists believe the vibrancy of those colors both help attract mates and ward predators.

name: Rattlejaw
description: Nefarian's reanimated bone constructs were an effective way to combat the many adventurers that plagued him, and especially their mages.

name: Twilight Clutch-Sister
description: Even very small things can be corrupted, the apocalypse is for everyone.

name: Bound Stream
description: Amongst the rudest of the Twilight's Hammer's corrupting activities was binding the elements of small streams and brooks.

name: Blightbreath
description: The eternal answer to the question 'what's that smell?'

name: Soulbroken Whelpling
description: The Lich King tore the spirits of Sindragosa's lost whelps into undeath in front of her skeletal form as a final affront to the great dragon.

name: Drudge Ghoul
description: One of innumerable ghouls summoned by the Lich King in his final battle, left to roam the world for eternity.

name: Wicked Soul
description: An unrepentant soul that was once trapped inside Frostmourne, and now flits restlessly between worlds.

name: Twilight
description: This energetic kitten changes colors depending on its mood.

name: Foe Reaper 0.9
description: The earliest prototype of the deadly Foe Reaper.

name: Nightmare Lasher
description: A lasher by any other name would smell as sweet.

name: Knight-Captain Murky
description: A tiny admirer of the Alliance, this courageous murloc made his own sword and shield to slay red-clad foes.

name: Ageless Bronze Drake
description: This particular drake has oddly retained its age throughout its travels, but its size has certainly changed.

name: Fel-Afflicted Skyfin
description: Watch out when it spits.

name: Transmutant
description: When alchemy goes wrong... So, so, wrong.

name: Untethered Wyrmling
description: A wyrmlings temperment hinges primarily on how long its been since its fed on mana. Remember to feed your pets.

name: Faceless Minion
description: It has no eyes, but it always seems to be staring at you.

name: Stardust
description: In the end, we are all wrought from stardust.

name: Dibbler
description: This tiny robot specializes in drilling, clamping, and shooting lightning out of its brain. A typical gnome invention.

name: Naxxy
description: It's just constantly barfing plague fluid. Where does it come from? Where does it go?

name: Scraps
description: He's one cowardly companion, because he's got no guts...

name: Ash'ana
description: One of Ash'alah's litter, entrusted to you by the Dreamweavers.

name: Tide Spirit
description: I wish that I could bind the tides and bend them to my will. But in its depths the sea abides and waits for no one still.

name: Dust Spirit
description: When it was captured and bound to a cage made of enchanted metal, this spirit managed to twist and reshape the metal into the suit of armor it now wears.

name: Blaze Spirit
description: This is the soul of a forest fire. Though its armor keeps its flames under control, you sometimes notice it gazing longingly at dead trees and dry underbrush.

name: Gale Spirit
description: When it was the wind, this spirit carried scents to every corner of Azeroth. Sometimes you can still smell on it the barest hint of food, smoke, or the carnage of battle.

name: Felclaw Marsuul
description: While most of the marsuul on Argus feed on fruits, this particular one prefers the juicy crunch of small rodents.

name: Fel Lasher
description: Beautiful and deadly.

name: Grasping Manifestation
description: Reaching out from some vast unknown corner of the void... to give you a hug.

name: Alliance Enthusiast
description: Maybe a bit too enthusiastic.

name: Lurking Owl Kitten
description: It's a bird! It's a cat! It's... biting me...

name: Zephyrian Prince
description: A minor diety of wind, lording over the tiny breezes that make you think the heat of the summer season will be bearable for a brief moment.

name: Hearthy
description: Crafted by a great and powerful wizard who refused to wear robes with pockets.

name: Young Mutant Warturtle
description: Mmm... Pizza!

name: Gleamhoof Fawn
description: Now, where are its parents? You monster.

name: Ghost Shark
description: Queen Azshara's curse over the denizens of Azsuna did not only affect the night elves. This shark is doomed to an eternity of bottomless hunger.

name: Fossorial Bile Larva
description: If a bile larva stays burrowed for an extended period of time they begin to lose their pigment. When they finally do emerge, they can be quite ravenous.

name: Benax
description: Younger brother to Anax.  Big brother to Cenax and Denax.

name: Noblegarden Bunny
description: Why do bunnies lay eggs?

name: Pocket Cannon
description: Mr. Smite kept this in his trunk, but always seemed to prefer his hammer. Seems like a mistake in hindsight.

name: Blazehound
description: Some say it's the pup of Riplimb and Rageface, others say 'I don't want to think about that.'

name: Cinderweb Recluse
description: A rarely-seen cinderweb species known for devouring her mate's legs and laying eggs in his still-twitching body, and a lovely purple color.

name: Surger
description: Five things he can never do without: surging, surging, surging, surging, surging.

name: Tylarr Gronnden
description: A terror so terrible, you're amazed it could ever be real.

name: Court Scribe
description: Ghosts are voracious readers.

name: Everliving Spore
description: In the fertile grounds of the Wailing Caverns, nothing truly dies.

name: Snowfang
description: A pup of Thrall's frostwolf, Snowsong, besting her in combat has earned you a lifelong companion.

name: Orphaned Felbat
description: Sure are a lot of these around the Broken Shore.

name: Infernal Pyreclaw
description: During his time in the Firelands, Fandral would often grow bored and work shaping magic on the local flora and fauna.

name: Faceless Mindlasher
description: Can't quite take over your mind, but capable of giving you a really bad migraine.

name: Bloodbrood Whelpling
description: Unlike other wyrms, blood wyrms drain their foes rather than consuming them whole.

name: Frostbrood Whelpling
description: Among the Death Knight's forces, the frost wyrm is the most formidable. This whelpling is no different.

name: Vilebrood Whelpling
description: Biohazard. Handle with gloves.

name: Ban-Fu, Cub of Ban-Lu
description: Introducing the youngest member of the Xuen Dynasty! Daughter of Ban-Lu. Niece of Xu-Fu. Granddaughter of the celestial Xuen... It's Ban-Fu!

name: Cavern Moccasin
description: This serpent seems used to being at your side, it may have been the companion of one of the other druids of the fang.

name: Young Venomfang
description: Untouched by the deviate corruption seeping through the Wailing Caverns.

name: Trashy
description: How do you kill that which lives in garbage?

name: Horde Fanatic
description: Puts the fan in fanatic!

name: Mischief
description: This ferocious feline followed the fel forces into Azeroth during the Legion's return.

name: Snowfeather Hatchling
description: Snowfeather falcosaur hatchlings instinctively flee to nearby adults when threatened. The species is particularly fond of large prey.

name: Direbeak Hatchling
description: Direbeak falcosaurs are heartier than other falcosaur species, but cannot smell as well. They prefer pungent meals and will often eat carrion that has been sitting in the sun for days.

name: Sharptalon Hatchling
description: Sharptalon falcosaurs can subsist entirely on a diet of small nuts and berries, and use their talons mostly for defense.

name: Nightmare Treant
description: Didn't play nice with the other plants in the nursery.

name: Lil' Coalee
description: No one knows how Lil' Coalee got his name. Was it his black heart? Was it his love for a warm fire? Was it simply that one of his parents were Big Coalee. Asking him yields no answers, only rage.

name: Albino Buzzard
description: Crysa has collected many exotic avians in her travels, none more rare than this hatchling.

name: Drafty
description: If you feel a slight gust that made you shiver, that was probably Drafty.

name: Squirky
description: During a special time of year, a secretive clan of murlocs often sends young tadpoles out to explore, adventure, and begin their tale.

name: Bloodgazer Hatchling
description: Though they may look fierce, the bloodgazer species of falcosaur has a beak that makes it predisposed to consuming fruits.

name: Tricorne
description: He'll plunder your provolone, turn your ship to swiss, then get your gouda. He's a true muenster and the scourge of the seven bries.

name: Lagan
description: Fond of slithering through tight spaces, these sea creatures lurk in the wreckage of sunken ships.

name: Wondrous Wisdomball
description: A magical wisdom-filled head in a ball bearing Khadgar's visage, it can only answer questions with a few phrases.

name: Cross Gazer
description: I always feel like somebody's watching me.

name: Stormstruck Beaver
description: This creature was once a normal beaver, but was struck by lightning, a stormwing drake's breath, and a rock all at the same time.

name: Tottle
description: Tottle's one wish is to explore the world at your side!

name: Rebellious Imp
description: What are they rebelling against? The real question is what are they NOT rebelling against.

name: Uuna
description: Uuna was found bound to an Ur'zul, and was released into the Shadowlands, where she wandered in the utter darkness.

name: Antoran Bilescourge
description: Bilescourge mostly feed on the corpses of victims of the Legion. However, since your arrival, it has developed a taste for demon corpses as well.

name: Flickering Argunite
description: Born from the crash site of the Xenedar on Argus.

name: Warpstalker Runt
description: While there are many similarities to the warpstalkers from Azeroth, evidence suggests they evolved completely independently from one another. Fascinating!

name: Voidstalker Runt
description: The void can consume life or meld with it. The latter often has unpercieved consequences.

name: Void Shardling
description: You have the odd feeling that it wants to kill you.

name: Arcane Gorger
description: Mana wyrm used to thrive on Argus many millennia ago, but lots has changed since those halcyon days.

name: Felcrazed Wyrm
description: If mana becomes scarce these wyrm look for other sources of energy to feed on.

name: Pygmy Marsuul
description: It likes to be petted behind the fins.

name: Skyfin Juvenile
description: Skyfin can fly from birth and there are some observed cases of mothers giving birth midflight!

name: Dart
description: This young crawgling was rescued from a vicious blood troll war camp. With care, and lots of feeding, you earned his trust and Krag'wa asked you to take care of him always.

name: Whomper
description: When Whomper wants to play, he'll let you know with a playful headbutt.

name: Cap'n Crackers
description: Don't say anything to Cap'n Crackers that you don't want repeated to you every day for the next 20 years.

name: Francois
description: Francois, a pompous member of the bockgeoisie, whose wealth and influence was born from the exploitation and labor of the peckletariat.

name: Silithid Mini-Tank
description: This silithid battle tank evolved prematurely. It will never reach full size, but it is just as ferocious as its larger cousins.

name: Spawn of Merektha
description: Not much is known about the great snake Merektha and the many eggs in the Temple of Sethraliss, but the residents of Zandalar all seem to have a distinct amount of both respect and fear for these serpents.

name: Filthy Slime
description: You're not entirely sure where this came from, nor do you really want to know, but you are sure that it does eat a tremendous amount of garbage.

name: Poda
description: Poda was just a small part of the larger business, always trying to live up to the standards of his older brother Coda.

name: Smoochums
description: Smoochums loved life in Glenbrook, it was full of people to play with and places to sleep, and also where unspeakable acts happen at the most innocent of tea parties.

name: Starshard Whelpling
description: Curious and inquisitive, this starry-eyed whelpling has big dreams and a wish to explore every world in the cosmos.

name: Lil' Ashlee
description: Lil' Ashlee quietly bides her time waiting for a power vacuum. Once in power Lil' Ashlee will bring fun, merriment and fire to all!

name: Lil' Tika
description: Most predators favored the soft parts of their prey to attack first.  Lil' Tika prefers to go for the face.

name: Direhorn Hatchling
description: The Direhorn species is among the toughest and strongest of all things that walk on Azeroth. They can seem like gentle giants, but be cautious that you don't find yourself in front of one when they are on a warpath.

name: Taptaf
description: Taptaf is loyal beyond a doubt, as long as food is provided.

name: Restored Revenant
description: The Drustvar people learned over time that when burying someone or some thing that there are sadly a number of shady people looking to exhume and reanimate whatever they can find.

name: Guardian Cobra Hatchling
description: A newly hatched Cobra can be just as deadly as a fully grown one, if not more.

name: Vengeful Chicken
description: The wisest engineers of Azeroth all know that the only thing more dangerous than a living evil chicken, is the mind of an evil chicken transplanted into the body of a mechanical chicken.

name: Enchanted Tiki Mask
description: The Zandalari are masters of magic and communications with the spirits, and they often imbued items with powers to strengthen their bonds with the spirit world.

name: Miimii
description: The mummy's wraps serves two purposes, one being to keep everything inside intact, the other is to lessen the fright of people seeing what's really below the wrappings.

name: Glutted Bleeder
description: Apply directly to the forehead.

name: Spectral Raven
description: Suddenly there came a tapping...

name: Boghopper
description: How many bogs has it hopped? No one knows.

name: Abyssal Eel
description: When the Naga invaded Stormsong, they brought their favored pets: Abyssal Eels.

name: Shadowback Crawler
description: Even the toughest of crabs with the toughest of shells can succumb to the dark mysteries of the shadows.

name: River Frog
description: The river frogs of Stormsong Valley have been known to try to hypnotize people by staring directly into their eyes and making a low rumbling noise.

name: Seabreeze Bumblebee
description: These hardy bees form the backbone of a great honey and mead industry. For decades, the Tidesages forbade anyone from taking them outside of Stormsong.

name: Corlain Falcon
description: Corlain Falcons are trained from birth to be scouts and fighting companions. The few spared from the Heartsbane curse serve the Order of Embers in the same capacity.

name: Freshwater Crawler
description: The hermit crabs of Stormsong Valley will use whatever they can find that fits them for their shell.  If it fits, they sits.

name: Vale Marmot
description: Vale Marmots are very quick on their feet and are masters of digging holes for their burrows.  Children like to chase them around and try to bop them on the head when they poke their head out to catch some sunlight.

name: Hermit Crab
description: When you're a hoarder, but want to stay on the move!

name: Coastal Bounder
description: A quite annoying species. No matter how high you build your walls, these creatures still manage to hop over.

name: Drustvar Piglet
description: This piglet avoided being turned into sausage at the hands of Roland with the help of a cunning spider.

name: Frenzied Cottontail
description: This red-nosed bunny is incredibly soft to the touch; just watch your fingers.

name: Child of Jani
description: The scavenger Loa thinks you can teach this saurid a thing or two. Just don't lose sight of your coin purse.

name: Swamp Toad
description: These toads do not appreciate having their legs pulled off.

name: Valley Chicken
description: Long ago, the population of chickens in Stormsong Valley was so high, there was fear that the chickens would eat all of the corn from the fields and leave the other residents hungry.

name: Sandyback Crawler
description: The sandyback crawlers get their name from having a slightly sticky outer shell that sand sticks to as they walk around.

name: River Otter
description: River otters start growing whiskers at a very young age and never stop.  By the time they are fully grown and reach old age, they have more whiskers on their face than any other known creature.

name: Bilefang Skitterer
description: Silver dust sparkling on each of this spider's legs attracts prey; unwittingly drawn into an inescapable web.

name: Greatwing Macaw
description: Best not to park your mount beneath this one.

name: Honey Bee
description: It is rumored that these honey bees can detect the pollen of a newly blossomed plant in northern Stormsong Valley when they are all the way down in Drustvar.

name: Parasitic Boarfly
description: The stinger of a Ravager Wasp can cause intense pain for up to three hours.  Consult a physician immediately if stung.

name: Shack Crab
description: Shack crabs are the only species of hermit crabs known to regenerate limbs if torn off in battle.

name: Baa'l
description: Not much is known about this otherworldly minion.

name: Mechanical Prairie Dog
description: This adorable mass of whirlers, cogs, and sprockets wasn't assembled in Gnomeregan. Where then?

name: Wicker Pup
description: She loves tummy rubs. Just watch out for splinters.

name: Inland Croaker
description: The mucus secreted from inland croakers is so effective at repelling water, the ship builders of Kul Tiras have been using it in the wood sealant for many years.

name: Giant Woodworm
description: The giant woodworm can burrow into almost any surface they crawl on, except solid stone.

name: Shore Butterfly
description: Butterflies taste with their feet! I tried it once, but I only made a mess.

name: Rooter
description: Built to dig holes by a gnomish farmer, Rooter quickly and predictably went haywire, with disastrous results.

name: Brutus
description: Built with ever-wagging tail technology, Brutus is a mechanically-certified good boy.

name: Tiny Direhorn
description: A popular pet among children, despite frequent accidental gorings.

name: Zandalari Shinchomper
description: Known to chomp on the shins of everything from saurids to brutosaurs.

name: Barrier Hermit
description: The 'door ditch' prank is not as effective when the house can chase you.

name: Coastal Scuttler
description: The purple coloration helps this species blend into the rocks and shadows, but the crabs become easy prey for seagulls when they linger too long on the open sand.

name: Golden Beetle
description: Many ancient tribes revered these enormous beetles.

name: Lil' Preyseeker
description: This wyrm can't wait to grow up big and strong so it can hunt Prey too!

name: Bloodfeaster Larva
description: Exceptionally squishy, especially after a big meal.

name: Tragg the Curious
description: Krag'wa reluctantly sent Tragg to the Talanji Expedition with the hope that they could find someone suitable to escort him on a tour of Azeroth.

name: Bloodfever Tarantula
description: While completely harmless to trolls, the animal life within Nazmir fear these creatures.

name: Elusive Skimmer
description: Water Skimmers use the tension of water and long, hydrophobic legs to help them walk along the water's surface.

name: Leafy Flutterwing
description: When perched on a tree, this species can be almost invisible to any prey.

name: Lil' Ben'fon
description: White Brutosaurs were on the brink of extinction until some enterprising adventurers took action. Lil' Ben'fon may have inherited her father's size, but definitely has her mother's temper!

name: Ranishu Runt
description: Always hungry, and has a shockingly good personality.

name: Young Sand Sifter
description: This species looks through the sand for the buried remains of rotting creatures. It keeps our beaches clean!

name: Sticky Oozeling
description: It's a repulsive creature. No one should want this.

name: Returned Hatchling
description: A terror in life. A terror in death.

name: Azerite Puddle
description: These drops of Azerite are volatile and prone to explosive reactions when prodded.

name: Fozling
description: A living piece of Arathi's most infamous stone-faced behemoth, Fozruk.

name: Sir Snips
description: No one knows who first started dressing up crabs in Azeroth, and it won't be stopping any time soon.

name: Bucketshell
description: No one knows who first started dressing up crabs in Azeroth, and it won't be stopping any time soon.

name: Fuzzy Creepling
description: The young of Arathi Highlands' plains spiders may kick clouds of barbed abdomen hairs into predators' eyes. All this, and poisonous web-spitters, too.

name: Ragepeep
description: Part owl, part bear... and part stag, which handily avoids potential lawsuits.

name: Lil' Siege Tower
description: A mighty war machine of adorable proportions.

name: Lil' War Machine
description: A mighty war machine of adorable proportions.

name: Saurolisk Hatchling
description: They are so cute before they develop razor-sharp teeth and venom.

name: Blue Flitter
description: So pretty you could eat it. But don't. It's poisonous.

name: Freshwater Pincher
description: Finding freshwater pearls with this crab by your side will be a pinch.

name: Crimson Frog
description: This frog's peculiar red color appears to have evolved specifically for camouflage in blood. Spooky.

name: Gruesome Belcher
description: The stench alone that this thing makes when it burps can easily ruin your day.

name: Barnacled Hermit Crab
description: She just wanted to get away. Find a nice shell. Sit on a beach. Now she's your pet.

name: Sandstinger Wasp
description: It is a well-established fact that wasps produce far higher quality honey than bees. Well-established in very, very small circles of wasp farmers.

name: Cou'pa
description: He is a real trooper, faithfully tagging along with Kojo for years. He enjoys hide and seek as well as some serious shell sliding.

name: Carnivorous Lasher
description: This horrific, flesh-eating plant promises it can make all of your wildest wishes come true. If you feed it.

name: Azeriti
description: A chip off the ol' girl's block. Azeriti is fiercely protective and will strike out against anyone wishing to do her mother harm.

name: Lost Platysaur
description: The platysaur species has been roaming around the lands since before time was even measured. An ancient species that has adapted to walking on the land, and also for some reason has adapted to repeating some short sounds three times in a row.

name: Scuttle
description: Scuttle was named after the ships he destroys, not his movement patterns, and don't you forget it.

name: Sparkleshell Sandcrawler
description: The shells of this particular group of crabs shimmer like gems on the sand.

name: Kindleweb Spiderling
description: The kindleweb spider species is known amongst the island tribes for its warm, threadable webbing.

name: Craghoof Kid
description: The hooves of this subgroup of goats have evolved to be equally surefooted on sand or stone.

name: False Knucklebump
description: Someone put this mechanical gorilla on the island to monitor the species, but who, and why?

name: Tinder Pup
description: The only balls this hound chases are diamond-hard, fireproof rocks. Very heavy, not fun to throw.

name: Bumbles
description: Bumbles may not have been the largest bee of the millions within the hive, but that doesn't mean he can't grow into a champion.

name: Bloodstone Tunneler
description: Is that a venomous worm in your pocket or... why is that in your pocket?

name: Poro
description: Poro cannot fly, but enjoys a long lifespan hopping along the ground and shaking the fruits from trees.

name: Squawkling
description: Boulderfist legend recounts a cloud of carrion birds following ogre warriors through the Dark Portal during the Second War; it may be that all two-headed scavengers in Azeroth are their descendants.

name: Juvenile Brineshell
description: Even young brineshell are drawn to the line in the sky that meets the waves, it calls them.

name: Kunchong Hatchling
description: Based on the size, the mantid appear to be re-growing their swarm, likely due to the presence of a new empress.

name: Gearspring Hopper
description: Mechanical Toads can hop over seven million times before their gears begin to wear out.

name: Slippy
description: These creatures are able to control each arm individually, which allows for great multi-tasking, and the ability to give the best hugs.

name: Captain Nibs
description: Captain Nibs was once the leader of an ill-fated sea voyage of slightly-too-smart-for-their-own-good monkeys that quickly capsized.

name: Barnaby
description: A wealthy pirate captain met with a strange man on the docks of an unknown city and bartered for everlasting life for his faithful companion. As with most monkey-related wishes, something went wrong.

name: Bloated Bloodfeaster
description: The smallest of cuts can be devastating to this species, they start to leak all over the place.

name: Baby Crawg
description: The horns of a crawg grow at a tremendous rate starting almost immediately after they are born, and is among the hardest of natural materials found on a living species.

name: Coldlight Surfrunner
description: The coldlight once were quite common across the oceans, to the grumbles of many hunters, but no longer.

name: Muskflank Calfling
description: Getting just one of these fragrant beasts damp is the olfactory equivalent to rolling around in a pile of wet dogs.

name: Snort
description: Particularly partial to coastal scrub grasses, this hardy species has survived many years in uncharted lands.

name: Voru'kar Leecher
description: The voru'kar are fiercely loyal to their leader, Vorus'arak, who led them to safety from the Lich King's grasp.

name: Inky
description: Whatever the story of his mysterious eyepatch is, he's not talking.

name: Octopode Fry
description: A baby octopode is technically referred to as a "squish."

name: Sandshell Chitterer
description: During the warm summer months, the noise that these pests make is enough to drive a person mad.

name: Deathsting Scorpid
description: It's sting is worse than... well nothing, really. Are you sure you want to keep this as a pet?

name: Accursed Hexxer
description: No one knows where this haunted tiki mask came from, or why its so angry all the time. It seems to like you though!

name: Foulfeather
description: Plaguefeather and her brood feed well on the carrion the battle for Stromgarde has left scattered across the Highlands.

name: Playful Frostkin
description: You know how disappointing it is when you return to your beverage and the ice has all melted? This guy's fault.

name: Giggling Flame
description: The most nefarious of the trickster spirits, and the reason why the pandaren invented fireproof pants.

name: Mischievous Zephyr
description: "Beware the cool breeze, for it brings trickery." - pandaren proverb

name: Laughing Stonekin
description: The earth that's rumbling beneath your feet... is it quaking or laughing, or both?

name: Dreadtick Leecher
description: The Dreadticks have grown to be a very invasive and dominant species wherever they wind up, they are incredibly quick to assault any new species until they find a weakness to exploit.

name: Aldrusian Sproutling
description: So long as one of his Sproutlings remains, Branchlord Aldrus will never truly die.

name: Voidwiggler
description: Either Darbel Montrose was a terrible warlock, or she simply adored cute things. Perhaps both.

name: Teeny Titan Orb
description: Constructs designed to assist the titans with sundry small tasks, this example is oddly small. Teeny Titan Orb! Let's Go!

name: Scabby
description: While Yogursa managed to survive the blight infesting many of Hillsbrad's ursines, she was driven mad, and her cubs forever tainted.

name: Thistlebrush Bud
description: The sprites that inhabit the islands of the Great Sea have thought of many traps to lure in unwary visitors.

name: Littlehoof
description: Littlehoof likes: bounding, bouncing, frolicking, and headbutts, in that order.

name: Snapper
description: Wear gloves with this little guy, he loves to bite!

name: Sunscale Hatchling
description: The bright scales of this rare wind serpent soak up the sunlight to keep it warm, even in shade.

name: Void Jelly
description: This Jelly has seen better days. Now it sees through fifty different eyes.

name: Zur'aj the Depleted
description: Once a mighty warrior of the n'raqi, Zur'aj's power was stolen and absorbed by an unknown rival.

name: Kor'thik Swarmling
description: Don't let these eat after midnight, legend has it that it's very dangerous.

name: Amberglow Stinger
description: The glow eminating from these is both calming and eerie at the same time when in total darkness.

name: Spawn of Garalon
description: The kunchong are extremely aggressive and will eat just about anything they can as soon as they are able to walk. If they can't eat it, they will smash it instead.

name: Ziggy
description: Every time the mandibles on this vibrate slightly, it is because it gave someone a nightmare.

name: Crypt Fiend
description: Six legs, two claws, multiple eyes, spiked body? This thing has it all!

name: Shrieker
description: Dogs can hear her coming from extremely far away.

name: Living Amber
description: The mantid are extremely reliant upon amber for many things, and even use it in some of their magic.

name: Ravenous Prideling
description: As a spawn of the most dangerous sha, this little monster has some big plans, but needs to wait a few thousand years first.

name: Happiness
description: This thing is equally as evil as it is adorable.

name: Anima Wyrmling
description: Even the smallest creatures in the Shadowlands rely on anima to survive.

name: Baby Zandalari Raptor
description: The raptors of Zandalar have their feathers at a very young age, making them seem like easy prey to an unsuspecting predator. In reality, these little terrors eat everything they want to and are prey to almost no other species.

name: Dasher
description: Like many other species, you can get a rough guess of how large the raptor will be as a fully grown adult by the size of its largest claw.

name: Wicker Wraith
description: The eerie magic in Drustvar has caused some unimaginable creatures to show up. Some still lurk in the shadows, others seem to be made out of shadows.

name: Trecker
description: One of the earliest recorded victories in a large battle on Zandalar depicted mighty warriors riding into battle on armored raptors. The legend has it that the warrior did not choose their raptor, but that the raptor would choose the warrior.

name: Tanzil
description: During certain times of the day, the sunlight can cause a reflection on the feathers of the red tinted raptors causing them to look more like flames than feathers.

name: Redridge Tarantula
description: The tarantulas that inhabit southern Redridge are smaller than their cousins elsewhere in Azeroth, but make up for their size with speed and ferocity.

name: Proper Parrot
description: This upper class parrot is only for the most distinguished of sailors.

name: Mechanical Cockroach
description: Mechanical Cockroaches are less likely to survive an apocalypse than an actual living cockroach, making this one of the more questionable feats of engineering.

name: Leper Rat
description: The radiation from all of the machinery in Gnomeregan gives these rats a soft green glow when looked at in absolute darkness.

name: Feathers
description: Thought to be lost in a massive explosion, the smartest of engineers were able to rebuild Feathers into a magnificent mechanical creation.

name: Child of Pa'ku
description: There is a story told to some children that all winged creatures were unable to fly until Pa'ku provided enough wind to lift them off of the ground.

name: Alarm-O-Dog
description: The Alarm-O-Dog was built for security purposes, but people really wanted one for their home because they are so darn cute.

name: Mini Spider Tank
description: Nobody is sure why someone thought making a miniature version of the machines in Gnomeregan was a good idea, but they sure are fun to play with.

name: Spawn of Krag'wa
description: A toad with Krag'wa's bloodline has an average expectancy to leap over seven million times during its lifetime.

name: Thunder Lizard Runt
description: The large plates on the back of this lizard are made of a mysterious but highly conductive material, making it ideal to charge up and expel massive amounts of electricity.

name: Nightwreathed Watcher
description: It is an honor in the Sentinel Army to be entrusted with the care of an owl born from the boughs of the great tree Teldrassil.

name: Mechantula
description: The Mechantula was created by an unknown gnomish Engineer. Rumor has it when the original schematic was found, it was in a room covered with dusty old webbing, but neither the original Mechantula or the engineer who drafted the schematic could be found.

name: Darkshore Sentinel
description: The Sentinel Owls of Darkshore are forever protective of their homeland.

name: Stoneclaw
description: Quilen were the favored companions of the ancient Mogu race due to their loyal nature.

name: Moon Darter
description: Since Amirdrassil's emergence, many faerie dragons have taken refuge in Belameth. But many others continue to call Kalimdor home.

name: Chillcrawler
description: Chill out. It's just a spider.

name: Wayward Spirit
description: Crossing into the spirit world too often can be dangerous for your health.

name: Comet
description: The Engine of Nalak'sha is defended by many mighty beings, and many tiny beings.

name: Gust of Cyclarus
description: When Cyclarus came to Darkshore, small cyclones appeared all over the place. Some of them dissipated over time, others found their way back to Cyclarus and were absorbed into the surrounding winds.

name: Detective Ray
description: This is what happens when you try to one-up Pip Quickwit's tall tales of being stuck inside of dangerous creatures.

name: Gillvanas
description: Little is known about the true intentions of this murloc, but her followers are among the most devoted.

name: Finduin
description: An unlikely leader of murlocs that had to step into the role after the death of his father, Murgian.

name: Baoh-Xi
description: For being an inanimate object for many centuries, this statue sure moves around really well.

name: Azure Windseeker
description: This little cloud serpent's favorite activity is chasing its own tail.

name: Spirit of the Spring
description: The elemental spirits of Pandaria have been known to plan tricks to play on unsuspecting visitors years in advance.

name: Hydrath Droplet
description: Hydrath Water Droplets are also an excellent skin moisturizer.

name: Everburning Treant
description: A lone Ancient of Lore in Darnassus managed to save this Treant in its final actions with a magical aura to prevent the flames from burning it any further.

name: Chitterspine Needler
description: A rare evolution of chitterspine, with elongated spines to pierce soft flesh and eyes.

name: Mr. Crabs
description: Mr. Crabs was victorious in a lengthy brawl amongst nearly a dozen other hermit crabs to claim his current shell.

name: Beakbert
description: Beakbert's favorite food is a ravenberry.

name: Froglet
description: The skin of a teal frog sometimes shimmers just right in the sunlight, providing excellent camouflage when on the surface of the water.

name: Papi
description: Legend has it that these purple butterflies came from a place called Devil's Island.

name: Sunsoaked Flitter
description: The wings of this butterfly start off with no distinct color, but slowly change to yellow as they spend time in the sunlight.

name: Crimson Skipper
description: This butterfly is stained red from the blood of its enemies.

name: Thunderscale Whelpling
description: These whelps are frequently captured by the denizens of Jorundall when still young and trainable.

name: Crimson Octopode
description: The crimson species of octopode grows larger and hardier than others, and is capable of sustaining crushing pressure in the depths.

name: Caverndark Nightmare
description: As if a dark, underwater cave wasn't nightmarish enough, these things exist -- and they love it there.

name: Scaley
description: Saurolisk scales are a surprisingly flexible but durable material that are often used to reinforce or mend things that are broken or worn through.

name: The Quackcestor
description: This is not your grandfather's duck! Or.. maybe it is?

name: Sophic Amalgamation
description: A small fraction of the titans' power, an even smaller fraction of their size.

name: Chitterspine Devourer
description: These gruesome crabs are attracted to the scent of blood and seem to revel in combat.

name: Daisy
description: Daisy moves at a very leisurely pace, and will happily accept a ride on your shoulders.

name: Junkheap Roach
description: Of all of the creatures in Mechagon, the roaches seem the most unaffected by all of the changes.

name: Experimental Roach
description: Experiments on this roach have grown it to an enormous size. Who would want that?

name: Fleeting Frog
description: The frogs of Mechagon had two choices when faced with mechanization: hide or breed. This species chose the latter.

name: Lightless Ambusher
description: In the lightless caverns, a bright shell color doesn't make you any worse at ambushing.

name: Minimancer
description: Some argue that the beard is what grants a spell caster their power, but they're wrong. The hat is where the magic is really from.

name: Yellow Junkhopper
description: The frogs of Mechagon had two choices when faced with mechanization: hide or breed. This species chose the former.

name: Motorized Croaker
description: The mechanical frogs of Mechagon Island have evolved to eat various mechanical insects, somehow.

name: Copper Hopper
description: The results of King Mechagon's experiments can be seen throughout the city and island.

name: Mindlost Bloodfrenzy
description: In the fathomless depths beyond the Eternal Palace, minds are lost in the blackness.

name: Rustbolt Clucker
description: Some of the few remaining fleshy creatures on Mechagon, they provide food and even companionship for the citizens of Rustbolt.

name: Mechagon Marmot
description: Experiments in mechanization have driven the few remaining critters in Mechagon to hide or flee.

name: Specimen 97
description: Specimen 97 escaped from his cage before being fully mechanized. Now, he seeks revenge.

name: Daggertooth Frenzy
description: Frenzies in Nazjatar have learned to operate in large schools or face consumption by the many predators of the deeps.

name: Nameless Octopode
