description: The influence of the old gods on the aquatic life of Nazjatar is unmistakable, as many simply wander as mindless eyes of N'zoth.

name: Amethyst Softshell
description: Spireshells produce a softer shell when young which will be grown out of quickly.

name: Void-Scarred Toad
description: Pulled from a horrific vision of Orgrimmar these toads are soaked in void energy.

name: Void-Scarred Pup
description: This pup is now more than just a bundle of fur and teeth, it is fur, teeth and void.

name: Alloyed Alleyrat
description: Though not real rats, they have taken over the back alleys of Mechagon City and carry diseased programs, earning them the name.

name: Scrapyard Tunneler
description: Natural burrowing tendencies were programmed into these critters, and now they perform vital functions breaking down the great piles of scrap.

name: Prismatic Softshell
description: Even with its softer juvenile shell, a young prismatic spireshell still glitters like a diamond.

name: Damplight Slug
description: The bright coloration of this deep water slug seems to give off a soft, warm glow.

name: Wriggler
description: Watch your ears.

name: Seafury
description: The elements clash and battle for relics constantly, but few moreso than the Tidestone.

name: Lil' Nefarian
description: This pocket-sized Lord of Blackrock is too small to call out any classes, but he can still dish up a good insult.

name: Jingles
description: These dazzling hatchlings have a distinctive coat color that has been highly sought after by leatherworkers for generations.

name: Sandclaw Pincher
description: The crabs of Nazjatar seem relatively unfazed by the sudden absence of the water.

name: Sandclaw Sunshell
description: The bright shells on these crabs makes them easy prey for predators, so they mostly stay to the shadows.

name: Glimmershell Scuttler
description: The bright shells of the hermit crabs of Nazjatar have the jewelry crafters of Azeroth buzzing with excitement.

name: H4ND-EE
description: When you need a hand, you know who to call.

name: Clanking Scrapsorter
description: The machinists of Mechagon may automate many of the tasks of the city, but they also spend a lot of time repairing and maintaining their inventions.

name: Stormwrath
description: The Tidestone would empower minions of both the storm and the sea, and is sought after by both parties.

name: Sandkeep
description: This pile of animated sand is fun to squish between your fingers, but doesn't really like it when you do that.

name: Scalebrood Hydra
description: The hydra matriarchs of Nazjatar steal and destroy one another's eggs constantly, so you can never really know what will hatch out of one.

name: Dottie
description: Don't be fooled by Dottie's soft coat and big eyes, her tiny, sharp teeth are for more than just munching on grass.

name: Chitterspine Skitterling
description: The chitterspine make a distinctive clicking noise when they see prey in the open.

name: Hissing Chitterspine
description: The "hissing" of this crab can be heard underwater, and is actually it rapidly clicking its smaller claws.

name: Great Sea Albatross
description: These albatrosses are slightly smaller than those found inland, and they can travel great distances over the sea.

name: Deeptide Fingerling
description: The deep sea frenzies of Nazjatar have long been a staple food of the naga.

name: Malfunctioning Microbot
description: Doesn't remember what it was originally programmed to do, just wants to battle other pets.

name: Twilight Whelpling
description: A re-emergence of whelps outside of Grim Batol has the red dragonflight worried -- something is happening in the lost city.

name: Zanj'ir Poker
description: Master of poking, stabbing, thrusting, jabbing, and gouging.

name: Brinestone Algan
description: Standing next to a brinestone algan will remind you of the smell of the sea, mixed with rotting kelp.

name: Budding Algan
description: There's no mistaking the evidence -- algan breed by budding. We can all put this argument to rest now.

name: Bonebiter
description: Absolutely do not let him bite your bones.

name: Bloodseeker
description: Some frenzies have been touched by whispers in the deep, and seek to turn the tides red with blood.

name: Spireshell Snail
description: The pressure of the crushing depths at which these snails exist help to form its distinctive shell.

name: Blackchasm Crawler
description: In the lightless trenches and chasms of the Great Sea, life still exists.

name: Chitterspine Deepstalker
description: Of the many frightening things that lurk in the deeps, a chitterspine nest is really up there.

name: Trench Slug
description: If you are diving into the deeps and one of these attaches itself to your leg, don't panic -- they're relatively harmless.

name: Abyssal Slitherling
description: The eels that inhabit the trenches and chasms of Nazjatar are a favored food of many creatures that inhabit the area.

name: Murgle
description: The murloc tribe that Murgle belongs to is one of few that have resisted the naga's enslavement.

name: Coral Lashling
description: The predatory coral of Nazjatar can grow incredibly quickly given the right conditions.

name: Wolf Balloon
description: A Darkmoon balloon made to look like a wolf.

name: Murloc Balloon
description: A Darkmoon balloon made to look like a murloc.

name: Horse Balloon
description: A Darkmoon balloon made to look like a horse.

name: Necrofin Tadpole
description: Legend has it that the Necrofin tribe found necromancy on a series of runed tablets at the bottom of the sea.

name: Skittering Eel
description: The eels of Nazjatar are experts at hiding in the many cracks and rocks of the sea floor.

name: Drowned Hatchling
description: When the waves crashed down around the city, not all were fortunate enough to receive N'zoth's gift.

name: Arachnoid Skitterbot
description: No one knows who made these mechanical spiders, but everyone hates them.

name: Echoing Oozeling
description: It doesn't take a very large ooze to eat a gnome.

name: Ghostly Whelpling
description: Unkillable dragons, what could go wrong?

name: Muck Slug
description: What else is there to say? Slugs love muck.

name: Kelpfin
description: Don't let the cuteness fool you, this little monster will jab your ankles the second you're not looking.

name: Golden Snorf
description: The birth of an exceptionally rare Golden Snorf was such an abnormality that not even King Mechagon could force himself to mechanize his beloved, fuzzy friend.

name: Snowsoft Nibbler
description: Of all snooters, this one has the softest fur. Ironically, it does not like to be pet very much.

name: Mustyfur Snooter
description: A rare snooter, and hard to find, it's known for burrowing itself deep under wet foliage to nest.

name: Adventurous Hopling
description: The last thing that tried to look in its tiny pack was never seen again.

name: Irradiated Elementaling
description: In case of darkness, this little elemental is an infinite source of a soothing green glow.

name: Slimy Otter
description: The slimy coating on this otter provides excellent protection against sunburns.

name: Rustyroot Snooter
description: Snooters have a very good sense of smell and can easily find grass and seeds buried under snow or scrap.

name: Duskytooth Snooter
description: The dark fur of the duskytooth allows them to more easily hide in the junkheaps of Mechagon Island.

name: Glittering Diamondshell
description: A rare species of crab found mostly in Nazjatar, it hides easily amongst the ample kelp and coral of the area.

name: Spawn of Nalaada
description: In a dark place unknown to all, the seabed writhes with the children of Nalaada, a carpet of life and death.

name: OOX-35/MG
description: Many advancements have been made to the initial prototype homing beacon over the years.

name: Lost Robogrip
description: It's not clear who the original owner of this arm was, but they're not getting it back now.

name: Slimy Eel
description: Eels are already slimy. This one is extra slimy.

name: Slimy Octopode
description: This little octopode squirts slime out instead of ink.

name: Slimy Fangtooth
description: A fully grown Fangtooth has a bite strong enough to go right through a platinum fishing pole. Watch your fingers.

name: Slimy Darkhunter
description: Living so deep within the seas has made this species very sensitive to lights.

name: Baby Stonehide
description: The playful kicks of these baby moose are enough to knock an orc flat on his back.

name: Leatherwing Screecher
description: We don't know much about this species, but we do know this: bats aren't bugs.

name: Rotting Ghoul
description: Get it while it lasts.

name: Sandclaw Nestseeker
description: Anything can be a nest, if you try hard enough.

name: Utility Mechanoclaw
description: As handy as this invention is, it was also programmed to absolutely despise hand puns.

name: Wailing Lasher
description: This lasher does not come from a black lagoon, or from the stars, or from beyond the moon. It just wants you to feed it.

name: Microbot XD
description: One of the microbots used to guard the city of Rustbolt, now purchasable for a reasonable price.

name: Slimy Hermit Crab
description: The slime on this provides essential nutrients to the hermit crab within, allowing it to go years without eating anything if it chooses.

name: Slimy Sea Slug
description: Slimy sea slugs secrete slimy sea slug slime on the slimy sea floor.

name: Rikki
description: Rikki has a talent for sneaking in and out of all manner of small spaces, if bribed properly with figs.

name: Needleback Pup
description: The needleback porcupines have survived life in island climates by evolving to be extra lucky.

name: Albino Duskwatcher
description: Albino ravens are incredibly rare and highly prized amongst the traders of the Great Sea.

name: Shadefeather Hatchling
description: Though barely a hatchling, its claws are already sharp enough to pierce the flesh of many smaller critters.

name: Lord Woofington
description: Lord Woofington was given his title after the rest of the denizens of Havenswood succumbed to madness.

name: Pearlescent Glimmershell
description: When the sun shines on its shell just right, the light scatters in a thousand different directions.

name: Microbot 8D
description: He's just so happy to be programmed to exist.

name: Spraybot 0D
description: Even he cannot put out the fire in my heart.

name: Armored Vaultbot
description: A walking vault that throws its money at you is not a very secure vault to own.

name: Rebuilt Gorilla Bot
description: The convenient part of robots that fight each other is that you can just put them back together. Step two is to attach a giant laser cannon to ensure you win the next battle.

name: Rebuilt Mechanical Spider
description: Anyone serious about using a mechanical spider in battle knows that you need to buy replacement legs in bulk. Those things are fragile.

name: Albatross Hatchling
description: The Albatross is capable of flying further than any other species of bird on all of Azeroth.

name: Firesting Buzzer
description: Firesting wasps are said to have one of the worst bites of any wasp species, causing a pain like a burning blaze.

name: Lustrous Glimmershell
description: A gift, found one day and saved for a meaningful occasion, should things ever turn around.

name: Jenafur
description: This cat is known for her lies, ignore her sweet meows.

name: Plagueborn Slime
description: In Maldraxxus, plague has a strange habit of finding its way back to you.

name: Burnout
description: Burnout always has a supply of well toasted marshmallows.

name: Crimson Bat Pup
description: Unlike most other bat species, the crimson bats are actually blind and rely entirely on echolocation to get around.

name: Cobalt Raven
description: The feathers of the cobalt raven glow eerily in the moonlight, making them easy to spot during the night.

name: Violet Abyssal Eel
description: The Abyssal Eels are able to swallow and digest something up to twice the width of their body.

name: Tonguelasher
description: It has one solution to all of its problems: give em' a lashing.

name: Scritches
description: Key activities include: rolling over, sunning self, whimpering for belly scratches, and enjoying belly scratches.

name: Brilliant Glimmershell
description: A gift, found one day and saved for a meaningful occasion, should things ever turn around.

name: Void-Scarred Hare
description: Only a being of immense power could scar such an adorable creature, unless this hare was already approaching the darkness...

name: Void-Scarred Cat
description: This cat eats anything from random scraps of food to strange glowing cubes of immense power. Be careful in front of its mouth.

name: Void-Scarred Rat
description: A recent debate has risen up about whether Void-Scarred rats are better or worse than plagued rats.

name: Anomalus
description: This ooze leaves behind a slimy trail as it moves that has a stench that would rival a beast rotting in the dunes of Uldum for a thousand years.

name: Void-Scarred Beetle
description: The shell on these beetles is quite brittle from the void interference, but it also has a strangely fast regeneration rate.

name: Reek
description: This little guy grows an additional eye somewhere on its body every year. It rhymes with...

name: K'uddly
description: His tentacles give you the best hug your brain has ever felt.

name: Corrupted Tentacle
description: Where did it come from, where does it go? Where did you come from, creepy tentacle?

name: Rotbreath
description: Dragonbreath Chili is nothing compared to the stench coming from the mouth of this thing.

name: Muar
description: The thirst for mana is insatiable, luckily this little one doesn't consume a whole lot.

name: Void-Scarred Anubisath
description: N'Zoth's corruption spread across many beings, including this little Anubisath.

name: Aqir Hivespawn
description: The aqir hive has countless numbers of beings in it, all of which are supportive of the ever-present Hivemind.

name: Ra'kim
description: The winged aqir are very fast and agile, and their wings provide a nice breeze on a warm afternoon.

name: Aqir Tunneler
description: These Aqir Scarabs are masters at tunneling through any terrain.

name: Pygmy Camel
description: These Pygmy Camels are often mistaken for Mysterious Camel Figurines.

name: Eye of Corruption
description: This creepy eye is looking at everyone, always, and only has to blink once every 10,000 years.

name: Gloop
description: It is very unclear whether this slime started with all of those eyes inside of it, or if those are "leftovers".

name: C'Thuffer
description: It is always C'Thuffer. Always.

name: Cursed Dune Watcher
description: This little guy was exiled and cursed by other watchers of Uldum to spend the rest of its time as a tiny insignificant being.

name: Wicked Lurker
description: Three legs, two arms, three eyeballs, the extras are for emergency situations only.

name: Jade Defender
description: Several adventurous miners have gone after the jade quilen in search of riches, but none have ever been heard from again.

name: Windfeather Chick
description: Despite what the young pandaren may tell you, the windfeather cranes do not actually have feathers made out of wind.

name: Oasis Void-Duster
description: Nobody is sure what sort of powder is coming off of these moths, and everyone is too terrified to find out what it actually is.

name: Void-Scarred Locust
description: Even some of the insects have succumbed to the void. They really do adapt and survive through everything.

name: Voidskimmer
description: These look especially magnificent when gliding over the water under a full moon.

name: Voidglower
description: Is the void glow in addition to their natural glow, or does it replace it? The world may never know.

name: Golden Chirper
description: The golden chirper has managed to stay its pristine self, despite corruption attempts from both the Sha and from the forces of N'Zoth.

name: Shadowbarb Hatchling
description: Void magic and giant flying insects, a combination nobody ever asked for. Surprisingly effective!

name: Chirpy Valeshrieker
description: Despite the adorable name, the chirp is more of a horrible, spine-tingling shriek.

name: Cloudfeather Fledgling
description: These ferocious flying creatures scrape their beaks against hard surfaces to sharpen them and intimidate foes.

name: Spriggan Trickster
description: Spriggans are tricky foes that live in Ardenweald and torment the Night Fae. This one has decided to play the ultimate trick--joining you.

name: Death Seeker
description: What horrors these eyes have seen in the tower of Torghast, with their unsleeping, unblinking gaze?

name: Gorm Harrier
description: If you count the number of times its wings beat per minute, you can calculate how long it will be until this little terror will sting you.

name: PHA7-YNX
description: Inspired by the phalynx of Bastion, Engineers have modified the design to be approximately 300% more adorable.

name: Amber Glitterwing
description: Much like how a squid can squirt ink to escape in the water, a glitterwing can shake the dust off of its wings and similarly vanish.

name: Fuzzy Shimmermoth
description: Shimmermoths don't actually shimmer in the sunlight. Nobody knows who named these, but they have a lot to answer for.

name: Starry Dreamfoal
description: When a full moon is visible, streaks of hair on the dreamfoal's mane start to shimmer and glow like shooting stars.

name: Spinebug
description: You got spinebugged!

name: Lost Quill
description: No longer lost, it's now considering a name change.

name: Glimr
description: A wandering adventurer saved a small band of murlocs that had lost their homes, and a glimmer of hope was born.

name: The Count
description: Roosting in the grounds of Castle Nathria, The Count did not foresee falling into an eternal stone slumber.

name: Sinfall Screecher
description: Joining with their new residents in Sinfall a few of these juvenile dredbats accompanied Renathal's forces into the maw.

name: Willowbreeze
description: When the winds blow just right, howling through the canyons of Ardenweald, you can find these foals running with the wind in a spectacular show of prowess and color.

name: Hungry Burrower
description: While these gormlings may not be the fastest diggers, they are very efficient and can move long distances underground before tiring.

name: Sunny
description:
All dogs go to Bastion. 

Cats go to Revendreth.

name: Dread
description: Staring at one of these creates a deep void of emotion at the very core of your being, then fills it with a sense of dread.

name: Sharpclaw
description: The razor sharp claws will tear into anything with ease, making for an excellent emergency can opener.

name: Ruffle
description: Plucking a feather from a steward is considered a sin worthy of a trip to Revendreth.

name: Jiggles
description: Though not very fuzzy, this slimy feline is still one of the most adorable things you've ever seen.

name: Grubby
description: These little slugs will try to eat literally anything they scoot over.

name: Gorm Rootstinger
description: The sting from these gorm is incredibly painful and often inflicts temporary paralysis, even on large targets.

name: Gorm Needler
description: There are more sharp pointy things on this species of gorm than there are on a Horde capital city.

name: Stoneskin Dredwing Pup
description: These dredbats are built by the Stonewright for the Court of Harvesters. They are bonded to their owners and become fierce fighters.

name: Rosetipped Spiderling
description: Identified by the signature coloring of their many legs, these spiders are known for elaborate dances used to threaten and confuse foes.

name: Devouring Animite
description: No one knows where the devourers came from, but it is theorized that they flourish on several lost land masses in the In-Between.

name: Bleak Skitterer
description: Legends say that if you see a bleak skitterer in your dreams, you are destined to battle one to the death.

name: Animated Cruor
description: These slimy creatures make the strangest suction noises when they try to climb up a wall. This is a well-known fact in Maldraxxus, for obvious reasons.

name: Ashen Chomper
description: Sometimes it is best to leave what strange creature you found in a mysterious jar in the depths of Torghast alone.

name: Maw Stalker
description: These guardians of Torghast are always watching for suspicious activity.

name: Tranquil Wader
description: Part of the wildlife of Ardenweald, they sometimes sleep while standing straight up in the water.

name: Necroray Spawnling
description: Toxic spores rain down wherever these creatures fly, turning small, unwary critters into a gruesome feast.

name: Pearlwing Heron
description: The whooping noise these pets make when hungry will rival any tormented cry you can find in Torghast.

name: Maw Crawler
description: The mysterious forces that animate this mobile mitt don't seem particularly evil, yet.

name: Nether Familiar
description: Arcane-infused wyrms are favored familiars of Eversong mages.

name: Vibrant Manaling
description: The mana wyrm population really picked up after the Arcane Torrent nerf.

name: Wrathful Wyrm
description: It's popping wings! Run!

name: Frenzied Mawrat
description: Its teeth are razor sharp and will chew through just about anything.

name: Lavender Nibbler
description: Poking a tiny gormlng in the side makes a very satisfying, squishy noise. It is NOT advisable to poke one too many times.

name: Bound Lightspawn
description: Lightspawn can always be found on the battlefields where light touches, no matter what form it takes.

name: Animated Tome
description: Beware awakening the power of knowledge.

name: Burdened Soul
description: A soul burdened with the sins of its past.

name: Torghast Lurker
description: That eerie feeling you get when the shadows seem to shift and follow you isn't just a feeling, it's usually one of these.

name: Will of Remornia
description: Remornia has been exposed to untold amounts of evil and sin, and will siphon power from any available source to fuel its desires.

name: Undying Deathroach
description: This species has a very distinct set of markings on its back that makes it easy to identify in the unending horrors of the Maw.

name: Lost Soul
description: A soul, having faced the sins of the past, wanders lost in the recent turmoil of the Shadowlands.

name: Primordial Bogling
description: The sludge that can give rise to the dredgers has been known to coalesce and run a muck.

name: Archivist's Quill
description: A good friend to Archivist Fane since his earliest days, Janeera is ready for him to let the quill go as she feels he is getting too attached.

name: Decaying Mawrat
description: Tiny flakes of skin seem to perpetually fall off of this horrible creature, leaving behind a trail and a very distinct, foul odor.

name: Wrathling
description: Drawn from an overabundance of pride and wrath these manifestations seek even more to sustain themselves.

name: Raw Emotion
description: These creatures manifest in the catacombs beneath Revendreth due to vile rituals practiced on souls there.

name: Spinemaw Gormling
description: While young, they have an adorable face and cute little antennae. While grown up, they gain razor sharp pinchers and are unstoppable eating machines. Life is full of compromises.

name: Renny
description: If you received points for causing mischief while teleporting around as a tiny adorable vulpin, Renny would be on top of the leaderboard.

name: Court Messenger
description: The stone fiends were created to serve as messengers and spies for the realm of Revendreth, and those of the Court of Harvesters serve that role extremely well.

name: Trapped Stonefiend
description: Trapped in a chest and forgotten for years, this stone fiend is mad and looking for a fight.

name: Dal
description: A fellow message courier to Chip, Dal was always on time until one day when he did not return from his appointed round.

name: Crimson Dredwing Pup
description: A wild dredbat, the crimson pups are tough to tame. Their elusiveness and hardiness set them apart from their kin.

name: Dusky Dredwing Pup
description: This dredwing is the most common of the dredbats, seen throughout the entire realm.

name: Mire Creeper
description: Hiding among the reeds near muck pools, juvenile mire creepers patiently waiting for easy prey to approach.

name: Withering Creeper
description: Subsisting on the residual anima in the waste of the Endmire, these mire creepers are withering away, yet in their famished state they are doubly dangerous.

name: Rocky
description: The claws of a vulpin sharpen themselves naturally when walking around on any sort of cobblestone or rocky surface.

name: Violet Dredwing Pup
description: A rare mutation gives this dredbat its violet underbelly, making it a sought after gift for pet collectors with eclectic tastes.

name: Dodger
description: Dodger is a bit small relative to other vulpin, but is excellent at playing hide and seek.

name: Floofa
description: Floofa smells like citrus fruits when its fur is wet.

name: Gloober, as G'huun
description: Even the terrifying G'huun was, perhaps, once this cute.

name: Vicious Leporid
description: Some leporids are docile and cute. Others are... still cute.

name: Riftblade Familiar
description: Rumored to be the familiar of a ren'dorei mage that escaped to the wilds of Voidstorm and became even more void infused.

name: Pernicious Bonetusk
description: The tusks on these creatures are fused to their skulls in such a manner that they feel no pain when charging or ramming into anything.

name: Boneweave Hatchling
description: Maldraxxus is full of mighty and terrifying creatures, causing most denizens to be abnormally calm when around the indigenous species of giant spiders.

name: Clinging Remains
description: Not quite alive, not quite dead, simply an arm, with no head.

name: Invasive Buzzer
description: Gorm find a way to invade just about anywhere, and the Maw is no exception.

name: Amaranthine Stinger
description: Though the spawn of a gorm queen lost within the Maw, this pet appears to be untainted by the Jailer's foul magics.

name: Bufonid Croaker
description: The signature song of the bufonid croaker can be heard across vast distances.

name: Invertebrate Oil
description: Is the ooze holding the bones in, or are the bones holding the ooze together?

name: Foulwing Buzzer
description: The barbed mandibles on this species will dig deep into anything they bite, causing a lot of irritation.

name: Golden Cloudfeather
description: A group of teroclaws is known as a fleece.

name: Writhing Spine
description: Some predators in Maldraxxus collect spines as trophies, and some of them don't stop moving even then.

name: Scurrying Mawrat
description: The nails of the mawrat can inflict one of several afterlife-ending illnesses.

name: Archetype of Discovery
description: One part facial sphere, one part cube manufactory, one part successful curiosity.

name: Silvershell Snapper
description: Terrapins prefer any sort of berry as their primary source of food, though they will eat almost anything.

name: Devoured Wader
description: When newly-hatched, they start with colorless, white feathers. Within weeks, they flourish into a wide spectrum of beautiful colors.

name: Scarlet Proto Avian
description: The distinctive red coloration of scarlet proto avians makes it difficult for them to hide from predators, which ironically means they're very hard to find.

name: Lost Limb
description: Fragments of souls abound in the Maw, searching for meaning, or an end to their torment. But some just want to give you a hand.

name: Archetype of Multiplicity
description: One part duplication, two parts duplication, four parts duplication... oops.

name: Winterleaf Spriggan
description: The allegiances of the spriggan are as changing as the seasons.

name: Stemmins
description: Whatever you do, don't ask it to carry a bucket of water. We don't talk about what happened to that area of the Shadowlands.

name: Battie
description: Roosting over Sinfall, Battie was roused one evening by the music of the first Ember Court. She has not missed a single one since.

name: Fledgling Teroclaw
description: Young teroclaws are surprisingly effective hunters and will attempt to bring down prey up to twice their size.

name: Helpful Glimmerfly
description: A glimmerfly can load up nearly fifty percent of its weight as pollen and still retain full agility while flying.

name: Vibrant Glimmerfly
description: The wings of glimmerflies are almost always saturated with some sort of pollen, causing a very steady flow of glittery dust to fall off of them as they fly.

name: Wild Corpsefly
description: Corpsefly eggs end up everywhere in Maldraxxus, and are easily transported accidentally.

name: Micromancer
description: Even the smallest bones don't go to waste in Maldraxxus.

name: Sludge Feeler
description: It is not advisable to hug a sludge feeler. Those stains never wash out.

name: Toenail
description: If you can beat them, stitch them!

name: Stony
description: Abandoned by its master in a secluded room, the little bat has been hibernating in stone for ages.

name: Fun Guss
description: This particular tiny tree is infested with strange spores and doesn't seem to mind.

name: Glimmerpool Hatchling
description: Contrary to popular belief, a terrapin's diet has nothing to do with its shell color.

name: Brightscale Hatchling
description: These brightly-colored terrapins are the least commonly seen in the wild. It is considered a sign of luck if you rub the top of its shell.

name: Luminous Webspinner
description: The bright color of the luminous webspinner taunts would-be predators to chase them into their webs.

name: Plaguelouse Larva
description: A parasite that latches onto some of the larger beasts of Maldraxxus and drinks until fully-grown, one wonders how the beasts don't manage to notice.

name: Leafadore
description: These saplings grow unnaturally quickly when given enough anima, and their leaves are razor sharp when fully grown.

name: Trootie
description: As long as any fragment of this species is found and planted, it will regrow into the same magnificent species as it once was.

name: Sable
description: Vulpin actually have very light-colored skin, but their incredibly thick coats hide any color from showing through.

name: Goldenpaw Kit
description: The golden vulpin are notorious for loud, barking yowls that echo across the plains of Bastion.

name: Purity
description: Despite having a very fine coat of fur, young vulpin do not shed at all.

name: Oonar's Arm
description: The thrill of battle and the grip of war have not yet left the bones of Oonar.

name: Corpulent Bonetusk
description: A newborn bonetusk has no visible tusks, but within weeks their tusks grow to a size proportional to their body.

name: Runelight Leaper
description: This pet has tremendous airborne agility and will always land on its feet, even when flung by a home-made spring-loaded leaper-flinger.

name: Sir Reginald
description: No one is quite sure where Reginald got his or her name, but what we do know is they don't answer to anything else.

name: Copperfur Kit
description: How is a vulpin different than a fox? That is a good question.

name: Gilded Wader
description: These golden-feathered creatures do not lay golden eggs, for that you would need a golden goose.

name: Indigo
description: The mane on this tiny foal reflects a majestic indigo color when the light hits it in just the right way.

name: Spirited Skyfoal
description: Few things are as adorable as a young skyfoal.

name: Shimmerbough Hoarder
description: These critters are capable of holding a tremendous amount of food in their cheeks for easy transportation between hiding spaces.

name: Rustfur Kit
description: Rarely, one of these kits is born to a golden-furred vulpin. It is considered a sign of distress in the pack.

name: Fluttering Glimmerfly
description: For some reason, these blue glimmerflies are often afraid of water.

name: Wader Chick
description: If you mat their silvery feathers down very carefully, it almost forms a mirror-like surface.

name: Wild Etherwyrm
description: These flying creatures do not leave feathers out in the wild. If a feather happens to ever detach from their body, the feather glows brilliantly for a moment and then vanishes from sight.

name: Corpselouse Larva
description: Though they are quite squishy while young, the bloodlouse will eventually grow hardened plates around its entire body.

name: Grip of Terror
description: These ensorcelled gauntlets are used by the mawsworn to capture escaping prisoners.

name: Grappling Gauntlet
description: Struggle all you want, it will just grip harder.

name: Irongrasp
description: There was once a mawsworn so vile that he was killed by his own minions. So great was his malice that a fragment of him lives on in this glove.

name: Deathroach
description: A species of wild pest that exists within the Maw, no one is quite sure what it eats or why it exists, but they can't get rid of it.

name: Verdant Kit
description: Raised in the verdant groves of Ardenweald, these kits are infused with the essence of rebirth.

name: Decay Grub
description: These grubs consume anima-depleted materials of all types. They aren't picky.

name: Starmoth
description: The wings of this moth glitter like the starry skies above Ardenweald.

name: Do, Child of Filo
description: Like his father, Do speaks little but is a friend to almost everyone except those who harm innocents.

name: Backbone
description: What it lacks in appendages, it makes up for in positive attitude.

name: Vile Deathroach
description: Every deathroach is technically considered vile, but these really take the cake.

name: Golden Eye
description: See reflections on the water? There's more than darkness in the depths.

name: Eye of Affliction
description: These eyes were sent to slow and debilitate the wayward souls of the Maw, so that they could be more easily captured.

name: Bubbling Pustule
description: The bubbles inside of these ooze creatures make a very satisfying pop noise when they finally surface and rupture.

name: Skittering Venomspitter
description: If this species spits venom at its prey, is it still technically considered venom, or just really gross?

name: Bloodlouse Larva
description: What vile subtance is jiggling around inside of this creature?

name: Deepwood Leaper
description: The latent energy found throughout Ardenweald's seeds sometimes augments small critters with unnatural abilities.

name: Chompy
description: One of several indistinguishable pets, all named Chompy.

name: Eye of Allseeing
description: The Eye of the Jailer, diffused, is now merely an all-seeing magical bauble.

name: Eye of Extermination
description: The rage Zovaal harbored for his punished existence echoes through the eye.

name: Gilded Darknight
description: The golden armor on these mawsworn is flimsy and easily bent -- the gilded armor is a punishment.

name: Timeless Mechanical Dragonling
description: Dragons come in all types of temporal classification -- timeless, time-lost, and even infinite.

name: Sly
description: Sly snuck aboard for a trip into the Maw. He's not scared, he just really wants to stay in your backpack for a while.

name: Animite Broodling
description: A young animite will rapidly grow if fed in excess. It is suggested that no more than one average soul's worth of anima be consumed per year to keep them docile.

name: Blinky
description: Blinky just hatched, and is ready for adventure, but may need a few naps along the way.

name: Murkastrasza
description: Hatch-queen of the baby murlocs, Murkastrasza spent many years battling her bitter rival, Deathy.

name: Mu'dud
description: The soul that occupies Mu'dud was a harsh ruler in life, and now he seeks to dominate the most violent kingdom of all.

name: Steward Featherling
description: Stewards looking to move up in steward society know to fluff up their feathers, give out tons of juice, and always be hooting.

name: Lost Featherling
description: Tales say that the stewards were created to aid the denizens of Bastion in their eternal duties. They don't seem to mind.

name: Larion Pouncer
description: Larion are prideful beasts, and see anyone that enters their territory as a challenge.

name: Mawsworn Minion
description: The Sanctum of Domination accepts mawsworn of all shapes, sizes, and levels of corporeality.

name: Mord'al Eveningstar
description: A ruthless tormenter of the smallest souls. Some say he was known in life as "Thermaplugg."

name: Rook
description: Rook never speaks, and little about them is known.

name: Anxious Nibbler
description: You have never met a pet that is more about chewing.

name: Iridescent Ooze
description: This slime seems to change color the more you look at it, and then suddenly, it appears to be looking back at you. Perhaps it is time for a rest.

name: Copperback Etherwyrm
description: The brilliant scales of the etherwyrm reflect the bright skies of Bastion.

name: Ruby Baubleworm
description: The gemstones on a baubleworm will quickly deteriorate if the worm is killed. Healthy worms are kept as prized pets by wealthy Brokers.

name: Turquoise Baubleworm
description: Baubleworms were found by the Brokers on an unknown world, and so gorgeous were their gems, that quickly the expedition turned against one another.

name: Tunneling Vombata
description: Only newly synthesized tunneling vombata have been seen. The rest are probably happily digging underground, never to be seen again.

name: Larion Cub
description: When fully-grown, a larion that has grown up with an aspirant will never leave his or her master's side.

name: Courage
description: Young larions are often given to aspirants as an early test of compassion and leadership.

name: Crawbat
description: For a pet that's all mouth, where does anything it eats go? Maldraxxus necromancers have dissected many of these pets, and have no answers.

name: Archetype of Animation
description: One part rocks, two parts bigger rocks, three parts smaller rocks, one part ability to move anyway.

name: Young Garnetgullet
description: The garnetgullet rolls in bright red flowers to enhance its color, serving as a call to similar species and a warning to enemies.

name: Korthian Specimen
description: With Korthia torn asunder by the Jailer, the few species that remain must be archived.

name: Topaz Baubleworm
description: Baubleworms have no natural predators, and grow their gemstones to attract mates.

name: Gurgl
description: New species of murloc are discovered around Azeroth all the time, owing to their invasive nature and varied diet.

name: Archetype of Motion
description: One part beak, two parts wings, one part inability to stop, one part unwillingness to stop.

name: Mosscoated Gromit
description: The hide color of this species is more rare on Korthia, and helps it hide from predators. And mawsworn, in this case.

name: Domestic Aunian
description: A domesticated species, it is quite calm and seems hungry.

name: Curious Purrkin
description: The Purrkin hunt the invasive skrats in the Veiled Market, keeping the population just high enough that they are still needed.

name: Silver Purrkin
description: Purrkins are a traditional pet of the Brokers and the two have a mutually beneficial relationship -- a necessity in Broker culture.

name: Devourling
description: Devourers exist throughout the In-Between of various types and levels of power, but always seeking anima.

name: Gnashtooth
description: It only has two teeth, and they both mean business.

name: Clutch
description: That place on your back you can't quite scratch? Get the help of this little guy. Problem solved.

name: Gizmo
description: Gizmo is considered a perfect purrkin, perhaps the best that has ever existed. He is also a ruthless murderer of small critters.

name: Damp Skrat
description: Skrats are poisonous, venomous, and unusually damp -- everything you want in a pet.

name: Feasting Larva
description: Being thrown into a pit of bloodlouse larvae is a painful fate for those that fail the great houses of Maldraxxus.

name: Shy Melvin
description: One of Plague Deviser Marileth's first creations, Shy Melvin was too timid for the demands placed on slimes by the House of Plagues.

name: Scavenging Skrat
description: Skrats are an especially invasive species, in that they can reproduce with only a sole skrat, and consume almost anything.

name: Rarity
description: One particular Broker has taken a liking to the skrat species, kept this rare specimin as a pet, and inherited the smell as a souvenir.

name: Fodder
description: When you're a Maldraxxian stitchcrafter and you have to name a lot of abominations, sometimes you just go with the obvious choice.

name: Lucy
description: Lucy was sent to Revendreth, as all cats are, and somehow escaped. When questioned, local dredgers blamed it on her being a cat.

name: Bloodfeaster Spiderling
description: Unlike most spiders that drain all the fluids from their prey, the bloodfeaster has developed a taste for blood alone.

name: Dredger Butler
description: Dredgers are the sweat (and sometimes tears) of Revendreth, and their help is absolutely critical to the workings of Revendreth.

name: Archetype of Metamorphosis
description: One part gills, one part haste, one part whatever it needs to be.

name: Resonant Echo
description: The core of a song that all but faded long ago.

name: Omnipotential Core
description: It could become anything. It could become *everything*. In the meantime, it's... this.

name: Geordy
description: It's hard to be sure since it has no face, but this thing seems to like you.

name: Archetype of Focus
description: One part focus, one part core, zero parts coherent or stable shape.

name: Char
description: The skies may have scorched part of Revendreth, but persistent creatures are still able to exist in these harsh conditions.

name: Pulsating Maggot
description: Normally just a plain white in color, their skin reflects whatever they have eaten and gains wonderful spots of color depending on the organs digested.

name: Drakks
description: A drakonid hatchling is a curious sight, but now is the time for curious things.

name: Sinheart
description: Sometimes sins of great souls are so powerful that they animate the very stones they are placed on.

name: Hissing Deathroach
description: Many have asked where these creatures came from, and how they made it to the Maw, but none that researched them have ever been heard from again.

name: Squibbles
description: Squibbles is engineered to never leave your side.

name: Flawless Amethyst Baubleworm
description: Baubleworms are highly prized in the Veiled Market, where those featuring few gemstone flaws can fetch extreme prices.

name: Blushing Spiderling
description: The crypts and tombs of Revendreth offer the perfect spider breeding ground, and Tomb Burster's brood has become fiercely territorial of their foothold.

name: Lil' Abom
description: The fields of battle teem with Lil'Abom parts, which are perfectly interchangable and easy to put back together.

name: Infused Etherwyrm
description: Infused etherwyrms can be used as portable anima batteries, but they must be trained not to flee at the sight of combat.

name: Dusty Sporeflutterer
description: The moths of Ardenweald can carry seeds and pods many times their own weight, aiding the Night Fae in their eternal tasks.

name: Archetype of Serenity
description: One part slippery skin, one part powerful legs, one part vocal sac, one part complete self-acceptance.

name: Venomous Bufonid
description: Some of the more adventurous denizens of Zereth Mortis consider venomous bufonids to be a delicacy.

name: Putrid Geist
description: Despite being decrepit and undead, geists move with exceptional speed and agility.

name: Carpal
description: Nobody knows why all of these skeletal hands are from the left hand. Where did the right hands go?

name: Tower Deathroach
description: Torghast is a tower of hardship and perpetual agony, which is a perfect environment for these creatures.

name: Severs
description: If a detached hand claps by itself inside of Torghast, can the Jailer hear it?

name: Timid Leporid
description: Some of the more timid leporids live deep underground. They emerge only to gather food before returning to their burrows.

name: Proto Avian Fledgling
description: While larger proto avians are known to feed on small animals, fledglings mostly eat insects and grubs.

name: Violet Violence
description: Some very clever wizard or dragon or other magical being thought they were being clever when they decided to color an ice elemental purple for a very elaborate bit of wordplay. They were probably right.

name: Pricklefury Hare
description: This is the most pettable pointy creature you will ever meet.

name: Magma Slug
description: Somehow this creature manages to be hot, rocky, and slimy all at the same time.

name: Shiverweb Broodling
description: Ironically, she has a warm personality.

name: Moon-Touched Netherwhelp
description: A strange whelp hatched on Netherwing Ledge, it is thought to be blessed by the waning lunar energies of Shadowmoon Valley.

name: Tarachnid Stalker
description: Some tarachnids prefer to lie in wait for their prey. These prefer to chase it, disable it, and devour it whole.

name: Archetype of Satisfaction
description: Two parts wings, one part beak, two parts talon, one part smugness.

name: Shelly
description: Isn't she just the sweetest little helicid?

name: Ambystan Darter
description: Ambystans are swift but cowardly creatures. At the first hint of danger, a darter will hide in the nearest hole.

name: Fierce Scarabid
description: The red coloring on this scarabid lets potential opponents know it is not to be trifled with.

name: Jeweled Onyx Whelpling
description: The master jewelcrafters of the Dragon Isles have perfected their craft so much that their gemmed statues can be brought to life. This whelp forged of obsidian is now not to be trifled with.

name: Ichabod
description: Ichabod began his existence as a prototype for the fearsome harvest golems terrorizing the farmers of Westfall, but a little gnomish ingenuity has since made him far less likely to attack your knees.

name: Buttercup
description: His pink antlers make him the life of any party.

name: Snowy Treeflitter
description: Nearly invisible, this creature leaps from tree to snow-covered tree.

name: Wakyn
description: Hope, like this phoenix, rises from the ashes of shattered dreams.

name: Lil' Maka'jin
description: Looks like Ben'jin and Maka'fon have kept busy since you last saw them. Maka'jin is ready for adventure but will settle for a large pile of leafy greens.

name: Buzzworth
description: Some species of red bees are known to lead solitary lives. Still, this busy bee seems happy to make time for you.

name: Garrlok
description: Ancient Gorian blood runs through Garrlok's veins. As far as anyone knows, only his diminutive stature keeps his imperial ambitions in check.

name: Crushhoof
description: Siege training begins early for kodo calflings that show a lack of inhibition towards breaking things. Mind your shins.

name: Tarachnid Ambusher
description: When prey wanders into this creature's reach, it rarely wanders out.

name: Archetype of Survival
description: One part shell. One part claw. As many parts resilience as need be.

name: Pippin
description: He has a heart of gold. Literally. These shiny good-boys are all the rage with affluent gnomes everywhere.

name: Ambystan Snapper
description: The ambystan snapper is one of the nicest creatures you can meet in Zereth Mortis... until it isn't.

name: Aurelid Floater
description: Most of Zereth Mortis' aurelids are content to swim, but some are born to fly!

name: Enraged Poultrid
description: Nobody calls this poultrid 'chicken.'

name: Predatory Helicid
description: When confronted, helicids will hide in their heavily armored shells. If provoked further, they will lash out with devastating force.

name: Gnomelia Gearheart
description: Spunky, funky, and just a little clunky -- she's a Gnomish engineer to a T!

name: Lightless Tormentor
description: This tiny tormentor of Torghast relishes in punishing any soul in the tower that can't step on it.

name: Drazka'zet the Wrathful
description: Fooled aeons ago by the clever loa of death into a bargain of unceasing service, this tiny ancient being carries out his duties eternally red with rage.

name: Archetype of Malice
description: Two parts wings, one part dagger, five parts sadism.

name: Momma Vombata
description: There are many terrifying things in Zereth Mortis, but none so terrifying (or adorable) as a vombata protecting its young.

name: Lil' Ursoc
description: Before he was known as one of the Wild Gods, Ursoc roamed the Grizzly Hills as a cub.

name: Egbob
description: Some hawkstriders are destined to be great. Others are destined to be Egbob.

name: Watcher of the Huntress
description: To its foes, this Watcher's innocent eyes belie the lethal danger of a prowling Huntress, waiting in the shadows.

name: Spyragos
description: This little dragon is all fired up! It looks like he's got some things to do.

name: Emmah
description: Is it normal for a fish to have fangs?

name: Patos
description: This frillfish is a no-frills kind of frillfish.

name: Kindlet
description: A troublesome mage named McGwire decided to start a small fire. He summoned a flame which caused him to exclaim: 'This is much more alive than required!'

name: Treeflitter
description: Many have tried to boop the cute little noses of creatures like this. Most have lost fingers.

name: Amber Treeflitter
description: The forests of Eversong have enjoyed a recent influx of these flying critters.

name: Pistachio
description: Pistachio got her name in exactly the way you would expect.

name: Bronze Racing Enthusiast
description: This little racing enthusiast has never missed a race and has never met a racer she didn't like.

name: Shyfly
description: These elusive creatures are famous for being hard to find unless you already know where to look. Fortunately, Haniko told you where to look.

name: Jeweled Sapphire Whelpling
description: Sometimes you can see blue light flickering deep inside the gems that make up this strange creature.

name: Berylmane
description: The vorquin of the dragon isles have manes that come in a variety of stunning colors.

name: Time-Lost Vorquin Foal
description: It's quite possible this creature is aging backwards. It looks a bit younger every time you see it.

name: Violent Poultrid
description: These poultrids are hostile, but never angry. They love every moment of every battle.

name: Multichicken
description: It's really unclear what's 'multi' about them.

name: Stabilized Geomental
description: Lihuvim's geomental creations are the most majestic of walking rock piles.

name: Shaggy
description: This little rabbit is cute from the tip of her button nose to the tips of her deadly claws.

name: Cubbly
description: Cubbly's extended proximity to time magic has given him powers far beyond the average bear.

name: Jeweled Amber Whelpling
description: The hardest part to shape was the whelpling's cute little snout.

name: Jeweled Ruby Whelpling
description: Trade Prince Gallywix wants you to know that he already owns three of these.

name: Driftling
description: This creature is usually known to be quite docile, but try to stick a carrot in its face and YOU WILL lose feeling in your fingers.

name: Vortex
description: Arrogant from a past life in the stormy Forbidden Reach, pines after the good old days when the elements controlled the land. Bit of a blow hard really.

name: Tremblor
description: Claims to be from a long lost land from the remnants of a massive volcano, but that story seems a little shaky.

name: Wildfire
description: The flames surrounding it burn more intensely during combat. Could even say that pet battles get it stoked.

name: Flow
description: Perks its head up when hearing conversation as it likes to stay current on world affairs.

name: Archetype of Cunning
description: Three parts planning, one part danger, one part noodle.

name: Dundun
description: [PH]

name: Naloki
description: With war paint on her fur and a growl in her belly, Naloki dreams of one day roaring like Nalorakk.

name: Red Viperid
description: The distinctive red coloration of these viperids serves as a warning to potential predators: 'eat at your own risk!'

name: King Viperid
description: Where does a 350-pound viperid sit? Anywhere it wants.

name: Green Viperid
description: Viperids can sit still for hours, silently awaiting their prey.

name: Jeweled Emerald Whelpling
description: A magically infused emerald sits at the heart of this creature.

name: Primal Stormling
description: Raindrops whip around this creature with frightening speed, ripping and tearing anything that touches it. Experts say the best way to avoid injury is to give it a treat and some snuggles.

name: Pocopoc
description: This isn't the real Pocopoc, but it provided this replica to you for use outside Zereth Mortis. Its details are so accurate as to be utterly convincing to anyone who hasn't spent lots of time with the real one.

name: Vombata Pup
description: Despite its vulnerable appearance, predators will often avoid attacking a juvenile vombata. It's not known exactly why, but many believe it has to do with the vombata's cute little nose.

name: Azure Frillfish
description: Oddly enough, you met this fish drifting through the air.

name: Percival
description: This potadpole is constantly getting stuck in things.

name: Lord Basilton
description: A brilliant executive assistant and apprentice, Lord Basilton also loves to stick his nose into piles of anything interesting.

name: Helpful Workshop Bot
description: The name 'Beepbop' followed by the words 'Made by Threadis' are roughly but lovingly scratched into the bottom plate on this tiny nerubian-crafted bot.

name: Lady Feathersworth
description: Though she may be fashionably late, Lady Feathersworth will always show up for a party.

name: Black Slyvern Pup
description: Above the vast green plains and rolling hills the slyvern fly, leaving only tiny shadows on the ground to mark their path through the sky.

name: Troubled Tome
description: Do you dare to open this book of draconic secrets?

name: Pinkie
description: With her distinctive coloration and impeccable work ethic, Pinkie has propelled herself to the top of the tallstrider fashion world.

name: Metallic Scarabid
description: Some of the smaller scarabids of Zereth Mortis have metallic outer shells to protect themselves. It also makes them look fabulous.

name: Bloodsucker Vespoid
description: Whether in life or the afterlife, some things are universal. Nasty blood-sucking insects are one of those things.

name: Snowclaw Cub
description: An adult Snowclaw can rattle the very trees with its roar. A cub can rattle one of the smaller branches.

name: Bugbiter Tortoise
description: This turtle eats bugs, as you'd expect from its name, but you wouldn't believe how large the bugs are.

name: Diamond Crab
description: Exposure to magic, minerals, and water pressure has left this crab looking very sharp.

name: Sapphire Crab
description: These crabs build their own shells from small fragments of rocks and gems.

name: Striped Snakebiter
description: These creatures were bred to hunt snakes. With a low profile and a long body, Snakebiters are perfect for chasing serpents into their own holes.

name: Roseate Hopper
description: Most brightly colored frogs are incredibly poisonous. The Roseate Hoppers are different because they are only credibly poisonous.

name: E'rnee
description: A little ambystan that was too big, then too little, and finally just right. He followed you home!

name: Blackfeather Nester
description: There is no fury quite like that of a mother Blackfeather defending her nest.

name: Magic Nibbler
description: It's tried to cut down, but magic is just so tasty.

name: Crimson Proto-Whelp
description: This proto-whelp's distinctive coloration is highly prized by pet trainers.

name: Black Skitterbug
description: After pestering the Dragonscale Basecamp for weeks, Granpap Whiskers discovered a way to catch them and use them to get rid of other more annoying insects.

name: Gray Marmoni
description: Under the invitation and tutelage of Doc Nanners many Gray Marmoni have become common, but not always desired guests at the Dragonscale Expedition Basecamp.

name: Phoenix Wishwing
description: Some think this creature is smoldering because it is on fire. In truth it's because of its burning gaze.

name: Ashenwing
description: Red for the fires in which it was born.

name: Gerald
description: Is it possible to rise from the ashes of time? Gerald thinks so and he will not be denied.

name: Igneoid
description: An Igneoid is formed when magma makes it to Azeroth's surface and is infused with dark elemental magic. The resulting creature is tiny, fierce, and very cute.

name: Palamanther
description: This salamanther wants to be your best friend.

name: Time-Lost Salamanther
description: This Salamanther met itself while lost in time. The two hung out on a rock together for a few hours. It was nice.

name: Echo of the Depths
description: What the deep claims, none can reclaim.

name: Ironbeak Duck
description: Though many creatures of the Dragon Isles are mysterious, the ducks are perhaps the most so. Where did they come from? Where are they going? What are they planning? We may never know.

name: Wild Duckling
description: Quack.

name: Pilot
description: Still learning how to fly.

name: Archetype of Renewal
description: Just one part jelly, but refreshed over and over and over again.

name: Terror Jelly
description: It feeds on fear, but is generally too cute to inspire terror on its own.

name: Prototickles
description: This creature's existence strongly implies the existence of other, more advanced tickle-based aurelids out there.

name: Leaping Leporid
description: Left to their own devices, these leporids will leap high into the air at the slightest provocation. Sometimes they'll even come down properly, as well.

name: Archetype of Vigilance
description: Two parts fortress, one part eyestalks, one part slime.

name: Viperid Menace
description: Where some viperids lie in wait for their prey, this variety stalks their intended meals for hours.

name: Microlicid
description: Since synthesized creatures come into being fully grown, why would anyone design an immature helicid?

name: Archetype of Predation
description: Eight parts legs. Eight parts eyes. One part rope. One part insatiable hunger.

name: Emerald Scarabid
description: This scarabid's brightly colored shell makes it more interesting to potential mates. Unfortunately, this also makes them interesting to potential predators.

name: Living Mud Mask
description: The greatest souvenir available in a dragon's spa in the clouds. Not so good at facials any more, though.

name: Mallard Duckling
description: What do you feed a cute little duckling? What? No, not cheese and quackers! Quackers aren't healthy for ducks.

name: Quack-E
description: Beep. Beep. Beep. Quack.

name: Plucky Duckling
description: This is the terror that flaps o'er the Dragon Isles. This is the winged nightmare that pecks at your emerald dreams. What? No it's not Deathwing, it's a duck!

name: Viridescent Duck
description: These beautiful, elusive birds have very erratic migratory patterns, which can be difficult to track, leading some explorers to erroneously believe ducks simply didn't exist.

name: Echo of the Heights
description: In the mountain air, frost lingers upon the grass as a storm rolls in.

name: Ohuna Companion
description: Its eyes can see the flick of a mouse tail from leagues away.

name: Grassland Stomper
description: Each of this creature's steps shakes the ground. When enough of them run together it sounds like a raging thunderstorm.

name: Lubbins
description: Who's the cutest little monster? You are!

name: Hoofhelper
description: Though it's rare, thunder lizards have been known to bond with centaurs. And their close allies. Lucky you!

name: Thunderfoot Calf
description: When these creatures stampede, they sound like a thunderstorm. When these creatures stampede in a thunderstorm, they sound like a cataclysm.

name: Blue Dasher
description: The bad news is that these bugs are carnivorous. The good news is that they usually just eat other bugs.

name: Polliswog
description: These critters often leave their spawning groups as soon as they can move around on land.

name: Yipper
description: Soon after you put down this slyvern's decay-twisted mother, you learned the hard way both how it got its name and why Zon'Wogi asked you to care for it.

name: Whitewhisker
description: These critters' white coats help them hide in the snow when  predators are around.

name: Scout
description: Scout came to the Dragon Isles for adventure and excitement. After finding you, he got both.

name: Petal
description: When the Bushbeard clan decided they were going to have a wedding, they chose Petal to be the flower girl. She was spectacular.

name: Alvin the Anvil
description: When Alvin said he wanted to be the best anvil ever, everyone doubted him. If anvils could laugh, he'd be the one laughing now.

name: Chestnut
description: Chestnut once found an acorn larger than he was, ate the whole thing, and doubled in size. It's a good thing he didn't eat a Trunkalumpf.

name: Grizzlefur Cub
description: These small bears will not normally attack people, but they have been known to steal the occasional picnic basket.

name: Razortooth Bear Cub
description: It's not easy having red fur during the winter, but in the autumn it's very fashionable.

name: Stoneshell
description: These creatures' shells are not literally made of stone, but they're strong enough that they might as well be.

name: Storm-Touched Ottuk
description: A playful splash from this otter will give you quite a shock.

name: Woodbiter Piculet
description: These birds use their sharp beaks and dragon-like talons to forage for prey on tree trunks and branches. They communicate with each other by drumming on the trees with their beaks, which makes a sound that can be heard over great distances.

name: Brightfeather
description: It has bright feathers. What else is there to say?

name: Secretive Frogduck
description: It looks like a duck and it smells like a duck and it quacks like a duck... what's a duck?

name: Crystalline Mini-Monster
description: A shard of a fragment of a piece of a sliver of a piece of crystal, but walking around like it owns the place. Good thing it likes you.

name: Mister Muskoxeles
description: Truly, the most dignified of stinky musken.

name: Emberling
description: The core temperature of an Emberling is hot enough to dissolve anything organic, so make sure to wear gloves if you want to pet it.

name: Chip
description: Born of living stone. Reforged by the hands of Wrathion's agents. Sadly much too tiny to make a difference in the black dragons' war against the djaradin.

name: Mister Toots
description: His name is Mister Toots because of the melodious sounds he can make with his trunk.

name: Ghostflame
description: A dream of the memory of fire.

name: Trunkalumpf
description: While their tiny tusks may not help much in a fight, the trunkalumpfs make up for it in sheer weight.

name: Echo of the Inferno
description: A spark can become an ember. An ember can become a raging fire. This creature was never a spark or an ember, it has only ever been a raging fire.

name: Bunbo
description: It sits anywhere it wants to, but it prefers to sit on laps.

name: Scruffles
description: Scruffles' lust for battle runs deep.

name: Pale Baby Vorquin
description: Behold! A pale vorquin.

name: Bakar Companion
description: Faithful and ferocious, this bakar is a hero's best friend.

name: Gilded Mechafrog
description: An old and dangerous discarded toy.

name: Backswimmer Timbertooth
description: When not lazily floating down a river, this Timbertooth is fond of snacks and snuggles.

name: Jean's Lucky Fish
description: To some, this fish speaks eloquently of its survival in the most arduous conditions. To most, it says 'glub.'

name: Vorquin Runt
description: It will probably grow bigger. Probably.

name: Kobaldt
description: Sharp crystal needles protrude from this creature's back, and yet it still wants you to pet it somehow.

name: Echo of the Cave
description: Formed by the reckless use of elemental magic, the slumbering earth awakens. It is angry.

name: Chalkshell Turtle
description: Despite its name, its shell is actually much tougher than chalk.

name: Azure Crystalspine
description: These creatures are highly sought after by hunters looking to make weapons from the sharp crystals on its back.

name: Crimsonspine
description: The blood of a crimsonspine flows through the clear crystal structures that make up its body, which causes its unique coloration.

name: Dusky Timbertooth
description: Hunters value these critters more for their companionship than their pelts.

name: Tiny Timbertooth
description: As good as gold and better. These little guys are quite frail but exceedingly brave. - Bob Bigheart

name: Mawtouched Geomental
description: No one in their right mind has ever seen a Mawtouched Geomental. Even so, plenty of people have seen Mawtouched Geomentals.

name: Storm-Touched Timbertooth
description: A timbertooth with lighting inside? Shocking!

name: Buckie
description: Gnawing at the roots of magic trees have given this creature the ability to transcend time itself.

name: Obsidian Proto-Whelp
description: After finding a clutch of black proto-whelps, Wrathion took them under his wing.

name: Scruffy Ottuk
description: Between battling, bathing, and playing, these ottuks have very busy schedules.

name: Swoglet
description: The reverberating sound of an adult hornswog's belch can be heard for leagues. Less far-reaching, but far more musical, is the dainty sound of a swoglet belching.

name: Snowlemental
description: When you feel a chill run down your spine, don't be afraid. It's probably just your pet snowlemental.

name: Whiskuk
description: Baby ottuks spend their days swimming, chasing butterflies, and practicing their intimidating roars.

name: Luvvy
description: In its big eyes you can see your own reflection. You also see everything else because its eyes are so big.

name: Arfus
description: There must always be a Lick King.

name: Puddlehopper
description: Puddlehoppers are known to eat bugs almost as large as they are. In one gulp!

name: Rock Martin
description: Rock martins make their nests out of scavenged pebbles.

name: Yellabon
description: When these insects move in a swarm they look like a golden fog.

name: Endmite
description: This creature usually has a muddy appearance, but occasionally you may find one that shines a brilliant red.

name: Roggy
description: Roggy is the kind of snail that never gives up, no matter how hard things get.

name: Rango
description: If bananas grew in caves, they would be Rango's favorite food.

name: Miloh
description: Miloh likes naps. Also snacks.

name: Blaise
description: Like a campfire, but it wants skritches.

name: Ambre
description: Nothing sparked joy in Ambre's heart until it found the perfect match.

name: Ysergle
description: Also known as the Dreamurk, Ysergle slumbered for many long years in the Emrgrled Dream.

name: Stonewhisker
description: The bright glow of a stonewhisker's ears is said to be the result of eating magical cheeses. This may also explain its extremely bad breath.

name: Cobbleshell
description: Slimy, yet also gritty. It would not make a good meal.

name: Boulderfang
description: Boulderfangs are known to make cozy nests in cabinets, shoes, and other inconvenient places.

name: Crimson Swoglet
description: Crimson swoglets are considered the most scholarly of all swoglets because they're well red.

name: Bronze Swoglet
description: Three years ago this swoglet was a fully grown adult.

name: Rusty
description: Rusty's powerful nose could smell a needle in a haystack.

name: Amador
description: Amador has a big heart and loves to help people, especially children and elders. He also likes belly rubs.

name: Brul
description: Sure it's just a rock, but how much can it lift?

name: Volatile Self-Driving Toolbox
description: Who needs a toolbelt when a handy robot can follow you around! Just don't bump into it too hard.

name: Azure Swoglet
description: Other frogs like to play, but this one is feeling too blue.

name: Scruffbeak
description: Hatched from a long-forgotten egg, this scruffy little eagle has decided you're its new flock, whether you're ready or not.

name: Slabwing
description: Despite being made mostly of rock, these creatures can fly. Some attribute this to the strength of their wings, but most agree it's probably just magic.

name: Hollow Moth
description: Not all moths spin silk. These moths spin lies.

name: Sunglow Cobra
description: A tiny sun seems to burn in its chest.

name: Lettuce
description: Cave swallows were once plentiful in the early days of Undermine's construction. Now, goblin construction has left the population of these beautiful birds dangerously low.

name: Byrn
description: Its insides are a raging inferno. Its outside is happy pup.

name: Jade Skitterbug
description: Imagine the size of the birds that eat these bugs.

name: Jerrie
description: Jerrie was once an ordinary rock mouse. He still is, in fact.

name: Diamondshell
description: These snails are especially prized by many drogbar snail-racers for being fast and looking fantastic.

name: Ravenous Shalewing
description: This creature can eat twice its bodyweight at a single meal.

name: Emerald Swoglet
description: Being green is... not easy.

name: Annuna
description: Annuna's distinctive coloration is due to her eating nothing but orange dragonflies.

name: Blub
description: Blub's heart has always been bigger than her tank, but once the rest of her got too big she had to find a new home. Fortunately, she can now travel with you and see all the marvels of the world!

name: Ebonwing Moth
description: Because they are often attracted to candles, these moths can be a big problem for kobolds.

name: Roland
description:
If Roland has taught you anything, it's that friendship comes at a price.

That price is lots and lots of cheese.

name: Iggy
description: Solid and dependable, Iggy is always ready for a fierce battle or a gentle metaphor.

name: Lord Stantley
description: Lord Stantley will arrive fashionably early to any party that serves shrimp.

name: Savage Lobstrok
description: It's not really all that savage, just a little crabby.

name: Cheddar
description: It got its name because its favorite food is fondue.

name: Bashful
description: While not as fast as some of the other snails in the Zaralek Cavern, Bashful makes up for it with sheer power.

name: Thimblerig
description: Known to hide money in its shell.

name: Tricky
description: Layers of rocky skin make Ambershells very tough to hurt.

name: Lithengale
description: The call of the lithengale is unique among birds in that it has a persistent, driving rhythm.

name: Scarlapod
description: Because their dens tend to be close to drogbar settlements, these snails are both faster and stronger than those on the surface.

name: Deepridger
description: The fire at the center of the world burns within its carapace.

name: Devourer Lobstrok
description: Fortunately it mainly devours pet treats.

name: Abyssal Lurker
description: A warlock won this little guy at an infernal carnival, but it quickly outgrew its aquarium.

name: Snapjaw Lurker
description: Like Neltharion, this fish went mad listening to whispers of the Old Cods.

name: Void Lurker
description: How exactly does one swim through a void? This creature knows, but isn't telling.

name: Paulie
description: This type of beetle feeds on dragon scales. Its bright coloration warns predators against attack.

name: Roseshell
description: Roseshells are known to be very agressive, but aren't very fast. So if you mess with one, prepare to be bitten in about five seconds.

name: Brulee
description: Somehow pretty and gross at the same time.

name: Ridged Shalewing
description: For a creature made of rocks it hovers quite effortlessly.

name: Soot-Stained Shalewing
description: These pests are known for their cantankerous attitudes and affinity for nesting in the smokestacks of Undermine, much to the locals dismay.

name: Whoopy
description: Delivers the goods while also looking good.

name: Lil' Manny
description: As master of all the ball pit lords, Manny rules over playgrounds with a spirit filled with fun and mischievousness.

name: Remembered Riverpaw
description: Once tenacious and terrible, the riverpaw slaughtered weaker enemies without hesitation.

name: Scruffyhorn Fel Snooter
description: Of all snooters, this one has the scruffiest and warmest fur. It is a very friendly snooter but use extreme caution when petting it.

name: Voidcrawler
description: Formented in the chaos and dust of Voidstorm. It hasn't even learned how to hate yet!

name: Mossy Snail
description: Imagine being so slow moving that moss grows on your back. Couldn't be me.

name: P.O.S.T. Assistant
description: While the markets of Tazavesh are a well-oiled machine, the P.O.S.T. service is a whirlwind of parchment, cartons, and rambunctious livestock. The use of P.O.S.T. assistants to handle dangerous material has saved a great many headaches.

name: Veridian Thorntail
description: This baby thorntail's favorite activity is splashing in small puddles.

name: Scrappy Thorntail
description: This little thorntail just loves to get into scraps and scrapes with its greatest foe: lapbugs.

name: Marrlok
description: Ancient Gorian blood runs through Marrlok's veins. As far as anyone knows, his desire to rest for long stretches of time on a sunny beach keeps his imperial ambitions in check.

name: Trishi
description: She sees you when you're sleeping.

name: Worgli the Apprehensive
description: Maybe give up! Maybe surrender!

name: Vile Bloodtick
description: Sweet is the black blood. It tastes of wars past, and wars to come.

name: Frenzied Bloodtick
description: Most people don't find congealed blood particularly appetizing. Bloodticks, though...

name: Smoldering Valor
description: With boundless energy and unbridled enthusiasm, the foal seeks the skies. It may not be ready, but exercise caution if you try to hold it back.

name: Dread Horrorling
description: Early attempts to integrate with the ecosystem resulted in multiple specimens being eyeblasted.

name: Impartial Watcher
description: This watcher's ennui is as eternal as the struggle between the Light and the Void.

name: Penumbral Terror
description: It waits and watches from the shadows. Softly. Softly.

name: Rhay'Dahr
description: Ever watchful, never blinking.

name: Looker Gaz'kreth Jr.
description: While not viewed as insightful enough to be a Watcher just yet, he's on the lookout for others to share his vision with.

name: Xanthous Siphonmite
description: Siphonmites generally feed on the energies of dying worlds, but this one is perfectly content with getting some scritches from researchers instead.

name: Jimmy
description: Jimmy's actual name is Morgilito, Spawn of Morgil, Chosen Enemy of Tazavesh, Devourer of K'aresh...but everyone just calls him Jimmy.

name: Cyan Siphonmite
description: How this siphonmite snuck its way into a stasis container is anyone's guess.

name: Vitriolic Inchshifter
description: Who knew these things could eat so much?

name: Palek'ti, the Mouth of Nothingness
description: Still not quite old enough to leave the nest.

name: Shimmering Inchshifter
description: Projections showed a less than one percent chance of successful integration, but this one is too cute to eliminate.

name: Tidal Kroling
description: These small krolusks seem to prefer the sandy shoals and high humidity of the isles' many beaches.

name: Wanderer Liu
description: Like most young meeksi, Liu has a penchant for finding both trouble and adventure. Unlike other meeksi, Liu can teleport across great distances in search of both.

name: Cinderwold Sizzlestinger
description: Now that is one spicy bee!

name: Pinkskin Burrower
description: So wrinkly!! So scrunchy!!

name: Happy
description: It doesn't seem to know where it is, but it's happy to be here!

name: Timbered Sky Snakelet
description: It is almost as if a wooden toy has come to life. Keep this pet away from children.

name: Razeshi B.
description: Razeshi B. is known for her short temper, quick wit and sharp barbs. The B. stands for "Best check yourself."

name: Claudius
description: Claudius is known for being brutally honest as well as a great singer. He knows your jewels would look better on him.

name: Silvermoon Broom
description: Magisters love to turn on their enchanted brooms before heading off to work, only to find it stuck in a corner of their Silvermoon apartment.

name: Dragonhawk Munchkin
description: This tiny breed of dragonhawk is popular for those who prefer to keep their companions travel sized. Just keep them away from curtains.

name: Belastrasza
description: Bela has learned some humility since you first met her, so she likely won't boss you around too much.

name: Tock the Clocker Spaniel
description: Tock the Clocker Spaniel is one of the best watch dogs you can ask for.

name: Remembered Construct
description: Made from the bits and pieces of vanquished abominations, light magic, and just a pinch of nostalgia.

name: Remembered Spawn
description: The faded memory of an Onyxian whelpling, this young dragon has decided that it wants to follow in its own footsteps.

name: Princess Vorquistrasza
description: Princess Vorquistrasza wants you to know that she appreciates you coming to her birthday party, but is underwhelmed by your gift.

name: Zhusadormu
description: Now that he's no longer sneezing through time, Zhusa is ready to accompany you on your future adventures! Or past adventures! Or future adventures!

name: Zalethgos
description: Zaleth is looking forward to accompanying you on your adventures. He's already got a list of books he wants to take with him, but Lasztak said he could only bring 2.

name: Pallibrius
description: Pallibrius is an amazing book because of its wealth of arcane knowledge. Also because of all the pie recipes its previous owner scribbled in the margins.

name: Posidriss
description: Posidriss is excited to go on adventures with you, but maybe let them have a nap first?

name: Somnius
description: Somnius is legendary for her ability to sleep through everything except suppertime.

name: Rustyroot Fel Snooter
description: Fel Snooters have a very good sense of smell. They will often immolate piles of leaves, scrap or snow to find grass and seeds.

name: Marmaduke
description: Though left behind on the Siren Isle, this old but very good boy proves that some things only get better with age.

name: Duskytooth Fel Snooter
description: The dark, fel-infused fur of the duskytooth allows them to more easily hide in the forests of Felwood.

name: Craboom
